beginner.question: Who is the mother of Jesus?
beginner.explanation: Mary was chosen by God to be the mother of Jesus. The angel Gabriel appeared to her and told her she would give birth to the Son of God.

beginner.question: In which town was Jesus born?
beginner.explanation: Jesus was born in Bethlehem. Mary and Joseph had travelled there because the Roman emperor ordered a census and everyone had to return to their family's hometown.

beginner.question: How many disciples did Jesus choose to follow Him closely?
beginner.explanation: Jesus chose 12 men to be His closest followers, known as the apostles. They travelled with Him, learned from Him, and later spread His teachings across the world.

beginner.question: At a wedding in Cana, Jesus performed His first miracle. What did He turn into wine?
beginner.explanation: When the wine ran out at a wedding feast in Cana, Jesus told the servants to fill jars with water. When they poured it out, it had become wine. This was Jesus' very first recorded miracle.

beginner.question: Who baptised Jesus in the River Jordan?
beginner.explanation: John the Baptist baptised Jesus in the River Jordan. When Jesus came up out of the water, the heavens opened, the Holy Spirit descended like a dove, and God's voice said 'This is my Son, whom I love.'

beginner.question: What is the first book of the New Testament?
beginner.explanation: The Gospel of Matthew is the first book of the New Testament. It tells the story of Jesus' life, starting with His family tree and birth.

beginner.question: Which disciple betrayed Jesus for 30 pieces of silver?
beginner.explanation: Judas Iscariot, one of the 12 disciples, agreed to hand Jesus over to the religious leaders in exchange for 30 pieces of silver. He identified Jesus to the soldiers by greeting Him with a kiss.

beginner.question: According to the Bible, how many days after His death did Jesus rise from the dead?
beginner.explanation: Jesus rose from the dead on the third day after His crucifixion, just as He had told His disciples He would. This event -- the resurrection -- is the foundation of the Christian faith.

beginner.question: Jesus miraculously fed over 5,000 people using just a small amount of food. What food was it?
beginner.explanation: A boy had five small loaves of bread and two fish. Jesus took them, gave thanks, and the disciples distributed the food to over 5,000 people -- with 12 baskets of leftovers.

beginner.question: In which city was Jesus crucified?
beginner.explanation: Jesus was crucified just outside the walls of Jerusalem, at a place called Golgotha (which means 'the place of the skull'). He had travelled to Jerusalem for the Passover festival.

beginner.question: Which disciple denied knowing Jesus three times before a rooster crowed?
beginner.explanation: After Jesus was arrested, Peter was asked three times if he knew Jesus, and each time he denied it. When the rooster crowed, Peter remembered Jesus' warning and wept bitterly.

beginner.question: What is the last book of the New Testament?
beginner.explanation: The book of Revelation is the last book of the New Testament and the entire Bible. Written by the apostle John, it contains visions about the end times and the ultimate victory of God.

beginner.question: What event does Easter celebrate in the Christian faith?
beginner.explanation: Easter celebrates the resurrection of Jesus Christ -- the day He rose from the dead, three days after His crucifixion. It is the most important celebration in the Christian calendar.

beginner.question: What event does Christmas celebrate?
beginner.explanation: Christmas celebrates the birth of Jesus Christ in Bethlehem. According to the Gospels, Jesus was born to Mary and placed in a manger because there was no room at the inn.

beginner.question: Who was Jesus' earthly father (His guardian who raised Him)?
beginner.explanation: Joseph was engaged to Mary and raised Jesus as his own son. He was a carpenter from Nazareth, and an angel appeared to him in a dream telling him not to be afraid to take Mary as his wife.

beginner.question: What was Jesus' job before He began His public ministry?
beginner.explanation: Jesus grew up in Nazareth where Joseph was a carpenter (or craftsman). Jesus would have learned and practised this trade during His early adult life before beginning His ministry around age 30.

beginner.question: On Palm Sunday, Jesus rode into Jerusalem. What animal did He ride?
beginner.explanation: Jesus rode into Jerusalem on a donkey while crowds laid palm branches and cloaks on the road and shouted 'Hosanna!' This humble entry fulfilled an Old Testament prophecy.

beginner.question: Which disciple refused to believe Jesus had risen until he could see and touch Jesus' wounds?
beginner.explanation: Thomas said he would not believe unless he could see the nail marks and put his hand in Jesus' side. When Jesus later appeared and invited him to do so, Thomas declared 'My Lord and my God!' This is why he is sometimes called 'Doubting Thomas.'

beginner.question: What was placed on Jesus' head before His crucifixion to mock Him?
beginner.explanation: The Roman soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and pressed it onto Jesus' head to mock Him as 'King of the Jews.' It was meant to cause pain and humiliation.

beginner.question: Who visited baby Jesus and brought gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh?
beginner.explanation: Wise men (also called Magi) from the East followed a star to find the newborn king. They brought three precious gifts: gold (for a king), frankincense (for God), and myrrh (foreshadowing His burial).

beginner.question: What was the name of the garden where Jesus prayed on the night before He was arrested?
beginner.explanation: Jesus went to the Garden of Gethsemane with His disciples on the night He was betrayed. There He prayed in deep anguish, asking God if there was another way, but ultimately accepting God's will.

beginner.question: How did Jesus die?
options: He was crucified on a cross
beginner.explanation: Jesus was crucified -- nailed to a wooden cross by Roman soldiers -- on a hill called Golgotha outside Jerusalem. Crucifixion was a common Roman method of execution at the time.

beginner.question: Who wrote many of the letters (epistles) found in the New Testament?
beginner.explanation: The apostle Paul wrote at least 13 of the 21 letters in the New Testament, including Romans, Corinthians, Galatians, and Philippians. He wrote them to encourage and instruct early churches.

beginner.question: Where did Jesus grow up as a child?
beginner.explanation: Although Jesus was born in Bethlehem, He grew up in the small town of Nazareth in the region of Galilee. That is why He is often called 'Jesus of Nazareth.'

beginner.question: Jesus drove people out of the Temple in Jerusalem. Why was He angry?
options: They were buying and selling things in God's house
They would not let Him in
beginner.explanation: Jesus found merchants selling animals and money changers doing business inside the Temple courts. He overturned their tables and said 'My house shall be called a house of prayer, but you are making it a den of robbers.'

beginner.question: On the night Jesus was born, who did angels appear to in the fields to announce His birth?
beginner.explanation: Angels appeared to shepherds watching their flocks at night near Bethlehem. The angel said 'Do not be afraid. I bring you good news of great joy -- today a Saviour has been born to you.'

beginner.question: Who was the Roman governor that sentenced Jesus to be crucified?
beginner.explanation: Pontius Pilate was the Roman governor of Judea. Although he said he found no fault in Jesus, he gave in to the crowd's demands and sentenced Jesus to be crucified.

beginner.question: Jesus taught His disciples a famous prayer. What is it commonly called?
beginner.explanation: The Lord's Prayer is found in Matthew 6:9-13. It begins 'Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name...' Jesus taught it as a model for how to pray.

beginner.question: Jesus calmed a storm while His frightened disciples were in a boat. On which body of water did this happen?
beginner.explanation: Jesus and His disciples were crossing the Sea of Galilee when a fierce storm arose. Jesus was asleep in the boat. When the terrified disciples woke Him, He simply said 'Quiet! Be still!' and the wind and waves obeyed.

beginner.question: In the parable of the Prodigal Son, what did the father do when his son came home after wasting all his money?
options: Welcomed him with open arms and threw a party
Made him work as a servant
beginner.explanation: The father ran to his son, embraced him, put a robe and ring on him, and threw a great feast to celebrate his return. Jesus told this parable to show how much God loves and forgives those who come back to Him.

beginner.question: When Jesus was born, where was He laid because there was no room at the inn?
beginner.explanation: Mary wrapped baby Jesus in cloths and placed Him in a manger -- a feeding trough for animals -- because there was no guest room available for them in Bethlehem.

beginner.question: What was Simon Peter's job before he followed Jesus?
beginner.explanation: Peter (also called Simon) was a fisherman on the Sea of Galilee. Jesus called him and his brother Andrew saying 'Come, follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.'

beginner.question: How many Gospels (books about Jesus' life) are in the New Testament?
beginner.explanation: There are four Gospels: Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. Each one tells the story of Jesus' life, death, and resurrection from a slightly different perspective.

beginner.question: Which of these is NOT one of the four Gospels?
beginner.explanation: Acts (or 'The Acts of the Apostles') is not a Gospel -- it is the story of the early church after Jesus ascended to heaven. The four Gospels are Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John.

beginner.question: Who appeared to Mary to tell her she would give birth to Jesus?
beginner.explanation: God sent the angel Gabriel to Mary in the town of Nazareth. Gabriel told her 'Do not be afraid, Mary, you have found favour with God. You will give birth to a son, and you are to call him Jesus.'

beginner.question: During the Last Supper, what did Jesus share with His disciples to remember Him by?
beginner.explanation: Jesus took bread, broke it, and said 'This is my body given for you.' Then He took a cup of wine and said 'This is my blood of the new covenant.' Christians still share bread and wine (communion) to remember Him.

beginner.question: According to the Gospels, who was the first person to find Jesus' tomb empty on Easter morning?
beginner.explanation: Mary Magdalene went to Jesus' tomb early on Sunday morning and found the stone rolled away and the tomb empty. An angel told her 'He is not here; He has risen!'

beginner.question: In the Christmas story, who told Joseph in a dream to name the baby 'Jesus'?
beginner.explanation: An angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said 'Do not be afraid to take Mary home as your wife. She will give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus, because he will save his people from their sins.'

beginner.question: What was Matthew's job before he became one of Jesus' disciples?
beginner.explanation: Matthew (also called Levi) was a tax collector before Jesus called him. Tax collectors were disliked because they often cheated people and worked for the Roman occupiers. Jesus calling him showed that no one is beyond God's love.

beginner.question: According to the Gospels, what did Jesus do when parents brought their children to Him?
beginner.explanation: When the disciples tried to keep children away, Jesus said 'Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.' He then blessed the children.

beginner.question: In one of His most famous miracles, what did Jesus walk on?
beginner.explanation: Jesus walked on the water of the Sea of Galilee to reach His disciples' boat during a storm. When they saw Him, they were terrified, but He said 'Take courage! It is I. Don't be afraid.'

beginner.question: Jesus raised a man from the dead who had been in a tomb for four days. What was this man's name?
beginner.explanation: Lazarus of Bethany was a close friend of Jesus. When Lazarus died, Jesus wept. Then He went to the tomb and called out 'Lazarus, come out!' -- and Lazarus walked out alive, still wrapped in burial cloths.

beginner.question: In which river was Jesus baptised?
beginner.explanation: Jesus was baptised by John the Baptist in the River Jordan. The Jordan is a major river in the land of Israel and an important location throughout the Bible.

beginner.question: According to the Gospels, about how old was Jesus when He began His public ministry?
beginner.explanation: The Gospel of Luke tells us 'Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry.' Before that, He lived quietly in Nazareth working as a carpenter.

beginner.question: What was baby Jesus wrapped in when He was born?
beginner.explanation: Mary wrapped baby Jesus in swaddling cloths -- strips of cloth that were traditionally used to snugly wrap newborn babies -- and laid Him in a manger.

beginner.question: What guided the Wise Men to find baby Jesus?
beginner.explanation: The Wise Men (Magi) saw a special star in the sky that they believed announced the birth of a new king. They followed the star from the East all the way to Bethlehem where it stopped over the place where Jesus was.

beginner.question: Jesus told a famous story about a kind stranger who helped a beaten man on the road. What is this story called?
beginner.explanation: In the Parable of the Good Samaritan, a man was robbed and left for dead. A priest and a Levite walked past without helping, but a Samaritan stopped, bandaged his wounds, and took him to an inn. Jesus told this to teach that everyone is our neighbour.

beginner.question: After Jesus rose from the dead and spent time with His disciples, what happened to Him next?
beginner.explanation: Forty days after His resurrection, Jesus ascended into heaven from the Mount of Olives while His disciples watched. Two angels appeared and told them Jesus would one day return in the same way.

beginner.question: Jesus said the two greatest commandments are to love God and to do what?
beginner.explanation: When asked which commandment was the greatest, Jesus said to love God with all your heart, soul, and mind, and that the second is like it: 'Love your neighbour as yourself.' He said all of God's law hangs on these two.

beginner.question: What did Jesus tell His followers to do just before He ascended to heaven?
options: Go and make disciples of all nations
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'Go and make disciples of all nations, baptising them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit.' This command is known as the Great Commission.

beginner.question: When Jesus was being arrested in the garden, what did Judas do to show the soldiers which man was Jesus?
beginner.explanation: Judas had arranged a signal with the soldiers — he would kiss the person they should arrest. He went up to Jesus and said 'Greetings, Rabbi!' and kissed Him. This is known as 'the kiss of betrayal.'

beginner.question: After being baptised, Jesus went into the wilderness to fast and pray. How many days did He spend there?
beginner.explanation: Jesus spent 40 days and 40 nights fasting in the wilderness. During this time, the devil came and tempted Him three times, but Jesus resisted each temptation by quoting Scripture.

beginner.question: When Jesus was baptised, the Holy Spirit came down from heaven and landed on Him. What did it look like?
beginner.explanation: When Jesus came up out of the water after being baptised, the Holy Spirit descended on Him in the form of a dove. At the same time, God's voice from heaven said 'This is my Son, whom I love.'

beginner.question: In a famous story, a short tax collector climbed a tree just to see Jesus as He passed through town. What was this man's name?
beginner.explanation: Zacchaeus was a chief tax collector in Jericho who was too short to see over the crowd, so he climbed a sycamore-fig tree. Jesus looked up and said 'Zacchaeus, come down immediately. I must stay at your house today.'

beginner.question: Pontius Pilate agreed to let the crowd have Jesus crucified. What did Pilate do with his hands to show he was not responsible?
beginner.explanation: Pilate took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd, saying 'I am innocent of this man's blood. It is your responsibility!' This gesture showed he didn't want to be blamed for Jesus' death.

beginner.question: When soldiers came to arrest Jesus, one of His disciples drew a sword and cut off a servant's ear. What did Jesus do next?
beginner.explanation: Peter drew his sword and cut off the ear of the high priest's servant. But Jesus told Peter to put his sword away, then touched the servant's ear and healed it — showing compassion even to those arresting Him.

beginner.question: 'Jesus wept' is one of the most well-known verses because it is the shortest in the Bible. Why was Jesus crying?
beginner.explanation: Jesus wept when He arrived at the tomb of His dear friend Lazarus. Even though Jesus knew He was about to raise Lazarus from the dead, He was deeply moved by the grief of Lazarus' sisters and the mourners.

beginner.question: Jesus healed 10 men who had a skin disease called leprosy. How many of them came back to say thank you?
beginner.explanation: Out of 10 lepers that Jesus healed, only one returned to thank Him — and he was a Samaritan. Jesus asked 'Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?'

beginner.question: When Jesus called His first disciples (Peter and Andrew), what did He say He would make them?
beginner.explanation: Jesus saw Peter and Andrew casting their fishing nets into the Sea of Galilee and said 'Come, follow me, and I will make you fishers of men.' They immediately left their nets and followed Him.

beginner.question: One of the most famous verses in the Bible is John 3:16. It begins with which words?
beginner.explanation: John 3:16 reads: 'For God so loved the world that he gave his one and only Son, that whoever believes in him shall not perish but have eternal life.' It is one of the most quoted verses in the entire Bible.

beginner.question: Jesus taught what many people call the 'Golden Rule.' What does it say?
options: 'Do to others what you would have them do to you'
'Money is the root of all evil'
beginner.explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus taught: 'So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you.' This simple rule about treating others the way you want to be treated is called the Golden Rule.

beginner.question: In the parable of the Lost Sheep, a shepherd had 100 sheep and one went missing. What did the shepherd do?
options: Left the 99 to search for the one lost sheep
beginner.explanation: Jesus told this parable to show how much God cares about every single person. Just like the shepherd left the 99 safe sheep to search for the one that was lost, God seeks out those who have strayed.

beginner.question: At the Last Supper, Jesus shocked His disciples by doing a task usually done by the lowest servant. What did He do?
beginner.explanation: Jesus wrapped a towel around His waist, poured water into a basin, and washed His disciples' feet. He told them 'I have set you an example that you should do as I have done for you' — teaching them about humble service.

beginner.question: After Jesus was buried, what was rolled in front of His tomb to seal it?
beginner.explanation: After Jesus' body was placed in the tomb, a very large stone was rolled across the entrance. On Easter morning, the women found the stone had been rolled away and the tomb was empty.

beginner.question: Before he became a Christian and changed his name to Paul, what was this famous apostle originally called?
beginner.explanation: Paul was originally called Saul. He persecuted Christians before his dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus, where Jesus appeared to him in a blinding light and changed his life forever.

beginner.question: On the road to Damascus, something dramatic happened to Saul (Paul) that changed his life. What happened?
options: A bright light from heaven blinded him and Jesus spoke to him
An angel gave him a sword
beginner.explanation: A bright light from heaven flashed around Saul, and he fell to the ground. He heard Jesus say 'Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?' Saul was blinded for three days until God sent a man named Ananias to restore his sight.

beginner.question: When King Herod heard that a new 'King of the Jews' had been born, what did he secretly plan to do?
beginner.explanation: King Herod felt threatened by the news of a newborn king and secretly planned to kill the child. God warned Joseph in a dream, so Joseph took Mary and baby Jesus and fled to Egypt to escape.

beginner.question: To escape King Herod's plot to kill baby Jesus, where did Joseph take Mary and the child?
beginner.explanation: An angel warned Joseph in a dream to flee to Egypt because Herod wanted to kill Jesus. The family stayed in Egypt until Herod died, then they returned and settled in Nazareth.

beginner.question: Jesus taught a famous sermon from a mountainside that includes the Beatitudes and the Lord's Prayer. What is it called?
beginner.explanation: The Sermon on the Mount, found in Matthew chapters 5 to 7, is one of Jesus' most important teachings. It includes the Beatitudes ('Blessed are the poor in spirit...'), the Lord's Prayer, and the Golden Rule.

beginner.question: When the devil first tempted Jesus in the desert, he told Jesus to turn what into bread?
beginner.explanation: After Jesus had fasted for 40 days and was very hungry, the devil said 'If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.' Jesus refused, saying 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from God.'

beginner.question: After Jesus fed over 5,000 people with five loaves and two fish, how many baskets of leftover food were collected?
beginner.explanation: After everyone had eaten their fill, the disciples collected 12 full baskets of leftovers — showing the incredible abundance of Jesus' miracle.

beginner.question: Jesus used many 'I am' statements to describe Himself. Which of these did He say?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.' This was one of several 'I am' statements where Jesus revealed His identity and mission.

beginner.question: When Jesus was baptised, a voice spoke from heaven. What did it say?
options: 'This is my Son, whom I love'
beginner.explanation: When Jesus was baptised and came up out of the water, God's voice from heaven said 'This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.'

beginner.question: Lazarus, the man Jesus raised from the dead, had two sisters. What were their names?
beginner.explanation: Mary and Martha were sisters of Lazarus. They all lived together in the village of Bethany and were close friends of Jesus. When Lazarus died, both sisters told Jesus they believed he would not have died if Jesus had been there.

beginner.question: A religious leader named Nicodemus came to Jesus at night. Jesus told him that to see God's kingdom, a person must be what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus told Nicodemus 'No one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again.' He was talking about a spiritual rebirth — starting a new life through faith in God.

beginner.question: Where was Jesus standing when He was taken up into heaven (the Ascension)?
beginner.explanation: Jesus ascended to heaven from the Mount of Olives, near Jerusalem. His disciples watched as He was taken up into a cloud, and two angels told them He would return in the same way one day.

beginner.question: On the day of Pentecost, the Holy Spirit came upon the disciples. What appeared above each of their heads?
beginner.explanation: On the day of Pentecost, the disciples heard a sound like a rushing wind, and what looked like tongues of fire appeared and rested on each of them. They were filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking in other languages.

beginner.question: When pregnant Mary visited her relative Elizabeth, what happened to Elizabeth's baby (John the Baptist) inside her?
beginner.explanation: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby inside her leaped for joy. Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit and said 'Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you will bear!'

beginner.question: The angel who appeared to the shepherds on the night Jesus was born started by saying what?
beginner.explanation: The angel said 'Do not be afraid. I bring you good news that will cause great joy for all the people. Today in the town of David a Saviour has been born to you; he is the Messiah, the Lord.'

beginner.question: While Jesus was on the cross, the sky went dark in the middle of the day. How long did the darkness last?
beginner.explanation: From noon until 3 o'clock in the afternoon, darkness covered the whole land while Jesus hung on the cross. This supernatural darkness lasted three hours.

beginner.question: Jesus had an inner circle of three disciples who were closest to Him. Who were they?
beginner.explanation: Peter, James, and John were Jesus' three closest disciples. They were present at special events like the Transfiguration and were the ones Jesus took deeper into the Garden of Gethsemane to pray.

beginner.question: What is the second book of the New Testament, right after Matthew?
beginner.explanation: The order of the first five books of the New Testament is: Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, then Acts. Mark is the shortest of the four Gospels.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the vine, you are the...' what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'I am the vine; you are the branches. If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing.' He was teaching that staying connected to Him is essential.

beginner.question: In Jesus' parable of the wise and foolish builders, what did the wise man build his house on?
beginner.explanation: The wise man built his house on rock, so when the storms came, his house stood firm. The foolish man built on sand, and when the storms came, his house collapsed. Jesus used this to teach about obeying His words.

beginner.question: People tried to trick Jesus by asking if they should pay taxes to the Roman emperor, Caesar. What was Jesus' famous reply?
options: 'Give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's'
beginner.explanation: Jesus asked them to show Him a coin and asked whose face was on it. They said 'Caesar's.' Jesus replied with this brilliant answer that teaches both civic duty and devotion to God.

beginner.question: What was written on the sign that was placed above Jesus on the cross?
beginner.explanation: Pontius Pilate had a sign written and fastened to the cross. It read 'JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.' The religious leaders objected, but Pilate said 'What I have written, I have written.'

beginner.question: Before His crucifixion, the Roman soldiers dressed Jesus in a robe to mock Him as a king. What colour was the robe?
beginner.explanation: The soldiers stripped Jesus and put a scarlet or purple robe on Him — purple being the colour of royalty. They were mocking Him as 'King of the Jews' before beating Him and leading Him to be crucified.

beginner.question: After Jesus died, a wealthy man offered his own tomb for Jesus' burial. What was this man's name?
beginner.explanation: Joseph of Arimathea was a wealthy member of the Jewish council who was secretly a follower of Jesus. He asked Pilate for Jesus' body, wrapped it in clean linen, and placed it in his own new tomb cut from rock.

beginner.question: Jesus spoke to a woman at a well, which shocked His disciples because she was a Samaritan. Where was this well?
beginner.explanation: Jesus met a Samaritan woman at Jacob's well in Samaria. Jews and Samaritans normally avoided each other, but Jesus spoke to her about 'living water' and revealed that He was the Messiah.

beginner.question: When Jesus fed the 5,000, how many loaves of bread and fish did He start with?
beginner.explanation: A young boy had five small barley loaves and two small fish. Jesus took them, gave thanks to God, and miraculously multiplied them to feed over 5,000 people with 12 baskets left over.

beginner.question: When Jesus died on the cross, what happened to the large curtain inside the Temple?
options: It was torn in two from top to bottom
beginner.explanation: At the moment Jesus died, the thick curtain in the Temple — which separated the Holy of Holies from the rest of the Temple — was torn in two from top to bottom, symbolising that everyone now had direct access to God.

beginner.question: When the crowd was given a choice between freeing Jesus or a criminal, who did they choose to release?
beginner.explanation: Pontius Pilate offered to release one prisoner as was the custom at Passover. The crowd chose Barabbas — a notorious criminal — and demanded that Jesus be crucified.

beginner.question: While hanging on the cross, Jesus prayed for the people who were crucifying Him. What did He ask God to do?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.' Even while suffering on the cross, Jesus showed love and forgiveness toward those who were putting Him to death.

beginner.question: When Jesus appeared to Thomas after the resurrection, what did Thomas say when he finally believed?
beginner.explanation: When Thomas saw Jesus' wounds, he immediately believed and exclaimed 'My Lord and my God!' Jesus replied 'Because you have seen me, you have believed; blessed are those who have not seen and yet have believed.'

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the light of the...' what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus declared 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.' This is one of His most famous 'I am' statements.

beginner.question: At the wedding in Cana, who told Jesus that the wine had run out?
beginner.explanation: Mary, Jesus' mother, noticed the wine had run out and told Jesus. He initially said 'My hour has not yet come,' but Mary told the servants 'Do whatever he tells you.' Jesus then turned water into wine.

beginner.question: How many days after His resurrection did Jesus ascend (go up) into heaven?
beginner.explanation: Jesus appeared to His disciples over a period of 40 days after His resurrection, teaching them and giving them instructions. On the 40th day, He ascended into heaven from the Mount of Olives.

beginner.question: Some friends brought a paralysed man to Jesus, but the house was too crowded. How did they get him to Jesus?
options: They lowered him through the roof
beginner.explanation: Four friends carried the paralysed man up to the roof, made an opening, and lowered him down on his mat right in front of Jesus. Jesus was so impressed by their faith that He healed the man.

beginner.question: In the parable of the mustard seed, Jesus compared God's kingdom to a tiny seed that grows into what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said the kingdom of God is like a mustard seed — the smallest of all seeds — but when planted, it grows into the largest of garden plants, big enough for birds to perch in its branches.

beginner.question: As Jesus was dying on the cross, what were among His final words?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'It is finished' just before He died. These words meant that His mission — to save humanity through His sacrifice — was complete. He had done everything He came to do.

beginner.question: The book that comes right after the four Gospels in the New Testament is called what?
beginner.explanation: The book of Acts (also called 'The Acts of the Apostles') comes right after John and tells the story of the early church — how the disciples spread Jesus' message after He ascended to heaven.

beginner.question: When Jesus was 12 years old, His parents lost track of Him during a trip to Jerusalem. Where did they find Him?
options: In the Temple, talking with teachers
beginner.explanation: After searching for three days, Mary and Joseph found the young Jesus sitting in the Temple among the teachers, listening to them and asking questions. Everyone was amazed at His understanding.

beginner.question: The crowd chose to release a criminal instead of Jesus. What was the criminal's name?
beginner.explanation: Barabbas was a notorious prisoner. Pontius Pilate gave the crowd a choice between Jesus and Barabbas. Stirred up by the religious leaders, the crowd shouted for Barabbas to be released and for Jesus to be crucified.

beginner.question: After meeting Jesus, Zacchaeus the tax collector promised to give how much of his possessions to the poor?
beginner.explanation: Zacchaeus stood up and said 'Look, Lord! Here and now I give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I have cheated anybody out of anything, I will pay back four times the amount.' Jesus said salvation had come to his house.

beginner.question: When Peter tried to walk on water like Jesus, he became frightened and started sinking. What did Jesus say to him?
options: 'You of little faith, why did you doubt?'
beginner.explanation: Peter stepped out of the boat and actually walked on water toward Jesus. But when he noticed the strong wind and waves, he became afraid and began to sink. He cried out 'Lord, save me!' and Jesus immediately reached out and caught him.

beginner.question: How many criminals were crucified alongside Jesus, one on each side of Him?
beginner.explanation: Two criminals were crucified with Jesus — one on His right and one on His left. One of them mocked Jesus, but the other defended Him and asked Jesus to remember him.

beginner.question: One of the criminals on the cross asked Jesus to remember him. What did Jesus promise him?
options: 'Today you will be with me in paradise'
beginner.explanation: The repentant criminal said 'Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.' Jesus replied with one of the most beautiful promises in Scripture: 'Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise.'

beginner.question: On Easter morning, who rolled away the heavy stone from Jesus' tomb?
beginner.explanation: There was a violent earthquake, and an angel of the Lord came down from heaven, rolled back the stone from the entrance of the tomb, and sat on it. The guards were so afraid they shook and became like dead men.

beginner.question: The word 'Gospel' means what?
beginner.explanation: The word 'Gospel' comes from an old word meaning 'good news.' In the New Testament, the 'good news' is the message about Jesus Christ — that God sent His Son to save the world through His life, death, and resurrection.

beginner.question: The title 'Christ' that is given to Jesus means what?
beginner.explanation: 'Christ' comes from the Greek word 'Christos,' meaning 'The Anointed One.' It is the Greek equivalent of the Hebrew word 'Messiah.' So 'Jesus Christ' means 'Jesus the Anointed One' — the promised Saviour.

beginner.question: Jesus described Himself as 'the bread of...' what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'I am the bread of life. Whoever comes to me will never go hungry, and whoever believes in me will never be thirsty.' He meant that He alone can satisfy the deepest needs of the human soul.

beginner.question: When Jesus raised Lazarus from the dead, what did He call out at the tomb?
beginner.explanation: Jesus stood before the opened tomb and cried out in a loud voice 'Lazarus, come out!' And the dead man walked out, still wrapped in strips of linen. Everyone who witnessed it was amazed.

beginner.question: In the parable of the wise and foolish builders, what did the foolish man build his house on?
beginner.explanation: The foolish man built his house on sand. When rain, floods, and wind came, the house on sand collapsed completely. Jesus told this story to show that anyone who hears His words but doesn't obey them is like that foolish builder.

beginner.question: Paul travelled to many different countries to tell people about Jesus. What are these trips commonly called?
beginner.explanation: Paul went on at least three major missionary journeys across the Roman Empire — through modern-day Turkey, Greece, and eventually to Rome — establishing churches and spreading the message of Jesus wherever he went.

beginner.question: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus said 'You are the salt of the earth' and 'You are the...' what of the world?
beginner.explanation: Jesus told His followers 'You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden.' He was saying that His followers should shine brightly for others to see through their good deeds.

beginner.question: After Saul (Paul) was blinded on the road to Damascus, how many days was he without sight?
beginner.explanation: Saul was blind for three days and did not eat or drink anything. Then God sent a man named Ananias to lay hands on him. Something like scales fell from Saul's eyes and he could see again.

beginner.question: Who was the first person to be killed (martyred) for being a Christian?
beginner.explanation: Stephen was a faithful follower of Jesus who was arrested and brought before the Jewish council. After giving a powerful speech about Jesus, the angry crowd dragged him out of the city and stoned him to death.

beginner.question: Jesus fell asleep in a boat during a terrible storm. When the scared disciples woke Him, what did He do?
options: He calmed the storm with His words
He steered the boat to shore
beginner.explanation: Jesus stood up and rebuked the wind and the waves, saying 'Quiet! Be still!' Immediately the storm stopped and everything was completely calm. The amazed disciples asked each other 'Who is this? Even the wind and waves obey him!'

beginner.question: Jesus healed people on the Sabbath (the Jewish day of rest). How did the religious leaders react?
options: They were angry because they said it broke the rules
They asked Him to teach them
beginner.explanation: The Pharisees and religious leaders were furious because they believed healing counted as 'work' and working on the Sabbath was forbidden. But Jesus said the Sabbath was made for people, not people for the Sabbath.

beginner.question: Jesus was born during the time of which king?
beginner.explanation: Jesus was born during the reign of King Herod the Great, who ruled Judea under Roman authority. When Herod heard about the birth of a new 'King of the Jews,' he tried to have the child killed.

beginner.question: In the parable of the Prodigal Son, what job did the younger son end up doing after he spent all his money?
beginner.explanation: After wasting all his inheritance, the son was so poor and hungry that he took a job feeding pigs — one of the lowest jobs imaginable for a Jewish person. He was so hungry he wished he could eat the pigs' food.

beginner.question: How many temptations did the devil present to Jesus in the desert?
beginner.explanation: The devil tempted Jesus three times: first to turn stones into bread, then to throw Himself off the Temple, and finally to worship the devil in exchange for all the kingdoms of the world. Jesus refused all three.

beginner.question: After His resurrection, Jesus told Peter to take care of His followers using what phrase?
beginner.explanation: Jesus asked Peter three times 'Do you love me?' Each time Peter said yes, Jesus responded 'Feed my lambs' and 'Feed my sheep' — meaning Peter should care for and lead Jesus' followers after He was gone.

beginner.question: Jesus once said 'Where two or three gather in my name, there am I...' what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus promised His followers 'For where two or three gather in my name, there am I with them.' This means that whenever believers come together to pray or worship, Jesus is spiritually present with them.

beginner.question: Who wrote the book of Acts, which tells the story of the early church?
beginner.explanation: Luke, who was a doctor and a companion of Paul, wrote both the Gospel of Luke and the book of Acts. Acts picks up where Luke's Gospel ends — with Jesus' ascension — and tells how the early church grew.

beginner.question: Paul and Silas were thrown in prison for preaching about Jesus. At midnight, what did they do?
beginner.explanation: Despite being beaten and chained in prison, Paul and Silas prayed and sang hymns to God at midnight. Suddenly a violent earthquake shook the prison, the doors flew open, and everyone's chains came loose.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the resurrection and the...' what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said to Martha (Lazarus' sister) 'I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die.' He said this just before raising her brother Lazarus from the dead.

beginner.question: What was the name of the island where the apostle John wrote the book of Revelation?
beginner.explanation: John was exiled to the small island of Patmos because of his faith. While there, he received visions from God about the future, which he wrote down as the book of Revelation.

beginner.question: In the Beatitudes, Jesus said 'Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit...' what?
beginner.explanation: In the Sermon on the Mount, Jesus taught 'Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.' The 'meek' are those who are humble and gentle, not aggressive or power-hungry.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will...' what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus taught 'Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.' He was encouraging His followers to pray with confidence and persistence.

beginner.question: What did Jesus say about children and the kingdom of heaven?
options: 'The kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these'
Children must wait until they grow up
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.' He valued children's simple trust and faith as an example for everyone.

beginner.question: In the parable of the sower, some seeds fell on good soil. What happened to those seeds?
options: They grew and produced a large crop
beginner.explanation: The seeds on good soil represent people who hear God's word, understand it, and live it out. These people produce fruit — 30, 60, or even 100 times what was sown. The other soils represent people who reject or forget the message.

beginner.question: How many brothers did Jesus have, according to the Gospels?
beginner.explanation: The Gospels name four brothers of Jesus: James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas. He also had sisters. These were younger siblings born to Mary and Joseph after Jesus' birth.

beginner.question: What does the name 'Jesus' mean?
beginner.explanation: The name 'Jesus' comes from the Hebrew name 'Yeshua,' which means 'The Lord saves' or 'God is salvation.' The angel told Joseph to name the baby Jesus 'because he will save his people from their sins.'

beginner.question: What did Jesus do just before He was arrested in the Garden of Gethsemane?
beginner.explanation: Jesus prayed so intensely that His sweat was like drops of blood falling to the ground. He asked God 'Father, if you are willing, take this cup from me; yet not my will, but yours be done.'

beginner.question: What is the famous love chapter of the Bible that says 'Love is patient, love is kind'?
beginner.explanation: 1 Corinthians 13 is often called 'the love chapter.' Written by Paul, it describes the qualities of true love: patient, kind, not envious, not boastful. It is one of the most popular readings at weddings.

beginner.question: What did Jesus say is the narrow path that leads to?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction, and many enter through it. But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life, and only a few find it.'

beginner.question: When Jesus healed the paralysed man lowered through the roof, what did He say first?
beginner.explanation: Before healing the man's body, Jesus first said 'Son, your sins are forgiven.' When the religious leaders questioned this, Jesus healed the man physically as well — proving He had authority to forgive sins.

beginner.question: What animal did the Holy Spirit look like when it descended on Jesus at His baptism?
beginner.explanation: The Holy Spirit descended on Jesus in bodily form like a dove. The dove is a symbol of peace and purity, and this moment marked the beginning of Jesus' public ministry.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through...' whom?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.' He was telling His disciples that He is the only way to God and eternal life.

beginner.question: Which part of the Bible tells the story of the early church and how Christianity spread?
beginner.explanation: The book of Acts (Acts of the Apostles) tells how Christianity spread from Jerusalem throughout the Roman Empire. It covers events like Pentecost, Paul's conversion, and the apostles' missionary journeys.

beginner.question: Jesus called Peter 'the rock.' What did He say He would build on this rock?
beginner.explanation: Jesus said to Peter 'You are Peter (which means rock), and on this rock I will build my church, and the gates of death will not overcome it.' This is one of the most important statements about the founding of the church.

beginner.question: Who said 'Behold the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world' when he saw Jesus?
beginner.explanation: John the Baptist said this when he saw Jesus coming toward him at the River Jordan. By calling Jesus the 'Lamb of God,' he was pointing to Jesus as the ultimate sacrifice for the sins of the world.

beginner.question: When Jesus healed the blind man named Bartimaeus, what had Bartimaeus been doing by the roadside?
beginner.explanation: Bartimaeus was a blind beggar sitting by the roadside near Jericho. When he heard Jesus was passing by, he cried out 'Jesus, Son of David, have mercy on me!' Jesus healed him and said 'Go, your faith has healed you.'

beginner.question: After Jesus ascended to heaven, who did the disciples choose to replace Judas as the 12th apostle?
beginner.explanation: The disciples cast lots (a form of drawing names) and Matthias was chosen to take Judas' place, bringing the number of apostles back to 12. This happened before the day of Pentecost.

beginner.question: In the story of the Good Samaritan, who were the first two people to walk past the beaten man without helping?
beginner.explanation: A priest and a Levite (a Temple worker) both saw the wounded man but crossed to the other side of the road without stopping. It was the Samaritan — someone the Jews looked down on — who stopped to help.

beginner.question: What did the Prodigal Son's father give him when he returned home?
options: A robe, a ring, and sandals
beginner.explanation: The overjoyed father told his servants 'Quick! Bring the best robe and put it on him. Put a ring on his finger and sandals on his feet. Bring the fattened calf and kill it. Let's have a feast and celebrate!'

beginner.question: On the day of Pentecost, after Peter preached, about how many people believed in Jesus and were baptised?
beginner.explanation: After Peter's powerful sermon on the day of Pentecost, about 3,000 people believed in Jesus and were baptised that very day. This was the explosive beginning of the Christian church.

beginner.question: Jesus said to love not only your friends, but also your what?
beginner.explanation: Jesus taught 'Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you.' This radical teaching was one of the most challenging things Jesus ever said — calling people to love even those who do them harm.

beginner.question: What were the very last words Jesus spoke to His disciples before ascending to heaven?
options: 'You will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you'
beginner.explanation: Jesus' final words before ascending were 'But you will receive power when the Holy Spirit comes on you; and you will be my witnesses in Jerusalem, and in all Judea and Samaria, and to the ends of the earth.'

beginner.question: Jesus was born in Bethlehem.
beginner.explanation: Yes -- Jesus was born in Bethlehem, a small town south of Jerusalem. Mary and Joseph had travelled there for a Roman census because Joseph was from the family line of King David.

beginner.question: Jesus had 12 disciples.
beginner.explanation: Jesus chose 12 men to be His closest followers. They are sometimes called the 'twelve apostles' and included Peter, James, John, Andrew, Matthew, and others.

beginner.question: Jesus turned water into wine at a wedding.
beginner.explanation: At a wedding feast in Cana, the wine ran out. Jesus told servants to fill six stone jars with water, and when they poured it out, it had become wine. This was His first miracle.

beginner.question: The New Testament is the first part of the Bible.
beginner.explanation: The New Testament is the second part of the Bible. The first part is the Old Testament, which covers creation, the history of Israel, the prophets, and more. The New Testament begins with the birth of Jesus.

beginner.question: Jesus walked on water.
beginner.explanation: Jesus walked on the Sea of Galilee during a storm to reach His disciples' boat. When they saw Him walking on the water, they were terrified until He told them not to be afraid.

beginner.question: Jesus was baptised by John the Baptist.
beginner.explanation: Jesus went to the River Jordan and was baptised by John the Baptist. When He came up out of the water, the sky opened, the Spirit of God came down like a dove, and God's voice said 'This is my Son, whom I love.'

beginner.question: Judas betrayed Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Judas Iscariot, one of the 12 disciples, betrayed Jesus by handing Him over to the religious authorities for 30 pieces of silver. He identified Jesus with a kiss in the Garden of Gethsemane.

beginner.question: Peter denied knowing Jesus three times.
beginner.explanation: After Jesus was arrested, Peter was asked three times if he knew Jesus. Each time, Peter said he did not. Then a rooster crowed, and Peter remembered Jesus' warning. He broke down and wept.

beginner.question: Jesus rose from the dead on the third day.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was crucified on a Friday and rose from the dead on Sunday -- the third day. His empty tomb was discovered by women who had come to anoint His body, and angels told them He had risen.

beginner.question: Jesus was born in Jerusalem.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was not born in Jerusalem -- He was born in Bethlehem. Mary and Joseph travelled to Bethlehem for the Roman census, and Jesus was born there and laid in a manger.

beginner.question: The last book of the New Testament is Revelation.
beginner.explanation: Revelation (also called the Apocalypse) is the final book of the New Testament and the entire Bible. It was written by the apostle John and describes visions of the end of the world and the return of Jesus.

beginner.question: Jesus fed over 5,000 people with five loaves of bread and two fish.
beginner.explanation: A boy offered five small loaves and two fish. Jesus gave thanks, broke the food, and the disciples distributed it to over 5,000 people. Everyone ate their fill, and 12 baskets of leftovers were collected.

beginner.question: Jesus grew up in a town called Nazareth.
beginner.explanation: After Jesus' family returned from Egypt, they settled in the small town of Nazareth in the region of Galilee. Jesus grew up there, which is why He is often called 'Jesus of Nazareth.'

beginner.question: Christmas celebrates the resurrection of Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Christmas celebrates the birth of Jesus, not His resurrection. Easter is the holiday that celebrates Jesus rising from the dead.

beginner.question: Easter celebrates the resurrection of Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Easter is the Christian celebration of Jesus' resurrection -- when He rose from the dead three days after being crucified. It is the most important event in the Christian faith.

beginner.question: Mary was the mother of Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Mary was a young woman from Nazareth who was chosen by God to be the mother of Jesus. The angel Gabriel appeared to her and told her she would give birth to the Son of God.

beginner.question: Joseph (Jesus' earthly father) was a fisherman by trade.
beginner.explanation: Joseph was a carpenter (or craftsman), not a fisherman. It was Jesus' disciples Peter, Andrew, James, and John who were fishermen before they followed Jesus.

beginner.question: Jesus was crucified on a cross.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was sentenced to death by the Roman governor Pontius Pilate and was crucified -- nailed to a wooden cross -- at a place called Golgotha outside Jerusalem.

beginner.question: Jesus rode into Jerusalem on a horse on Palm Sunday.
beginner.explanation: Jesus did not ride a horse -- He rode a donkey. This humble choice fulfilled an Old Testament prophecy and showed that He came in peace, not as a conquering warrior.

beginner.question: The Wise Men brought gifts of gold, frankincense, and myrrh to baby Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Wise Men (Magi) from the East followed a star to find the newborn Jesus. They presented three gifts: gold (fit for a king), frankincense (used in worship), and myrrh (a burial spice foreshadowing His death).

beginner.question: Jesus calmed a storm on the Sea of Galilee.
beginner.explanation: While crossing the Sea of Galilee with His disciples, a violent storm arose. Jesus was sleeping in the boat. When the terrified disciples woke Him, He stood up and commanded the wind and waves to be still -- and they obeyed immediately.

beginner.question: Paul wrote many of the letters in the New Testament.
beginner.explanation: The apostle Paul wrote at least 13 of the 27 books of the New Testament. These are letters (epistles) written to churches and individuals, such as Romans, Corinthians, Galatians, and Philippians.

beginner.question: Matthew was a fisherman before he followed Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Matthew was a tax collector, not a fisherman. Tax collectors were disliked because they worked for the Roman Empire and often cheated people. Jesus called Matthew to follow Him anyway, showing His love for everyone.

beginner.question: Jesus taught a prayer known as 'The Lord's Prayer.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus taught His disciples how to pray with a model prayer that begins 'Our Father, who art in heaven, hallowed be thy name...' It is called the Lord's Prayer and is one of the most recited prayers in the world.

beginner.question: John the Baptist was Jesus' earthly father.
beginner.explanation: John the Baptist was not Jesus' father -- Joseph was Jesus' earthly father. John the Baptist was a prophet and a relative of Jesus (their mothers were related). He prepared the way for Jesus' ministry and baptised Him.

beginner.question: There are four Gospels in the New Testament: Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John.
beginner.explanation: The four Gospels -- Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John -- are the first four books of the New Testament. Each tells the story of Jesus' life, death, and resurrection from a unique perspective.

beginner.question: Jesus was tempted by the devil in the desert.
beginner.explanation: After being baptised, Jesus spent 40 days fasting in the desert, where the devil tempted Him three times. Jesus resisted every temptation by quoting Scripture.

beginner.question: A crown of thorns was placed on Jesus' head before His crucifixion.
beginner.explanation: Roman soldiers twisted together a crown of thorns and pushed it onto Jesus' head to mock Him as 'King of the Jews.' This was part of the suffering He endured before being crucified.

beginner.question: Jesus healed blind people during His ministry.
beginner.explanation: Jesus healed several blind people throughout the Gospels. One well-known example is Bartimaeus, a blind beggar who called out to Jesus by the roadside. Jesus said 'Go, your faith has healed you,' and immediately Bartimaeus could see.

beginner.question: Pontius Pilate sentenced Jesus to be crucified.
beginner.explanation: Pontius Pilate was the Roman governor of Judea who presided over Jesus' trial. Despite saying he found no fault in Jesus, Pilate gave in to the crowd and ordered Jesus to be crucified.

beginner.question: Jesus was raised as a child in the town of Bethlehem.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was born in Bethlehem but was raised in Nazareth. After a brief time in Egypt (fleeing King Herod), Mary and Joseph settled in Nazareth, where Jesus grew up.

beginner.question: Jesus performed His first miracle at a funeral.
beginner.explanation: Jesus' first miracle was at a wedding, not a funeral. At the wedding feast in Cana, He turned water into wine when the hosts ran out.

beginner.question: An angel told Mary she would give birth to the Son of God.
beginner.explanation: The angel Gabriel was sent by God to Mary in Nazareth. He told her 'You will conceive and give birth to a son, and you are to call him Jesus. He will be great and will be called the Son of the Most High.'

beginner.question: The disciples were afraid when they saw Jesus walking on water.
beginner.explanation: When the disciples saw Jesus walking toward them on the Sea of Galilee, they were terrified and cried out in fear, thinking He was a ghost. Jesus said 'Take courage! It is I. Don't be afraid.'

beginner.question: Shepherds visited baby Jesus on the night He was born.
beginner.explanation: After angels announced Jesus' birth to shepherds in the fields near Bethlehem, they hurried to find the baby. They found Mary, Joseph, and the baby lying in a manger, just as the angel had described.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'Love your neighbour as yourself.'
beginner.explanation: When asked about the greatest commandment, Jesus said to love God with all your heart and to love your neighbour as yourself. He said all the Law and the Prophets hang on these two commandments.

beginner.question: The book of Acts tells the story of Jesus' birth.
beginner.explanation: The book of Acts tells the story of the early church after Jesus ascended to heaven -- not His birth. The birth of Jesus is told in the Gospels of Matthew and Luke.

beginner.question: Peter was one of Jesus' closest disciples.
beginner.explanation: Peter was one of Jesus' inner circle of three closest disciples (along with James and John). He was often the first to speak, and Jesus called him 'the rock' on which He would build His church.

beginner.question: Jesus was about 30 years old when He began His public ministry.
beginner.explanation: The Gospel of Luke tells us 'Jesus himself was about thirty years old when he began his ministry.' Before that, He lived in Nazareth and worked as a carpenter.

beginner.question: Jesus never performed any miracles.
beginner.explanation: Jesus performed many miracles throughout His ministry -- healing the sick, giving sight to the blind, feeding thousands, calming storms, walking on water, and even raising the dead.

beginner.question: The angel Gabriel appeared to Mary.
beginner.explanation: God sent the angel Gabriel to Mary in Nazareth to tell her she had been chosen to give birth to Jesus, the Son of God. Mary responded 'I am the Lord's servant. May it be to me as you have said.'

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the way, the truth, and the life.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus spoke these words to His disciple Thomas during the Last Supper. The full statement is: 'I am the way and the truth and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.'

beginner.question: Bread and wine were shared at the Last Supper.
beginner.explanation: At the Last Supper -- His final meal with the disciples -- Jesus took bread and wine and told them to eat and drink in remembrance of Him. This act is the basis for the Christian practice of communion.

beginner.question: Jesus was buried in a cave-like tomb carved out of rock.
beginner.explanation: After the crucifixion, a man named Joseph of Arimathea placed Jesus' body in his own new tomb, which had been cut out of rock. A large stone was rolled across the entrance.

beginner.question: The New Testament contains 27 books.
beginner.explanation: The New Testament has 27 books in total: 4 Gospels, the book of Acts, 21 letters (epistles), and the book of Revelation.

beginner.question: Jesus raised a man named Lazarus from the dead.
beginner.explanation: Lazarus had been dead for four days when Jesus arrived at his tomb. Jesus called out 'Lazarus, come out!' and Lazarus walked out alive, still wrapped in burial cloths. This was one of Jesus' most dramatic miracles.

beginner.question: Jesus was baptised in the River Nile.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was baptised in the River Jordan, not the Nile. The River Nile is in Egypt. The River Jordan flows through Israel and is where John the Baptist was baptising people.

beginner.question: Jesus told many parables (stories with a lesson).
beginner.explanation: Jesus was a master storyteller who used parables -- simple stories with deeper spiritual meanings -- to teach about God's kingdom. Famous parables include the Good Samaritan, the Prodigal Son, and the Sower.

beginner.question: Paul was one of the original 12 disciples who walked with Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Paul was not one of the original 12 disciples. He became a follower of Jesus after a dramatic encounter on the road to Damascus, well after Jesus' death and resurrection. Before that, he had been persecuting Christians.

beginner.question: After His resurrection, Jesus appeared to His disciples before ascending to heaven.
beginner.explanation: After rising from the dead, Jesus appeared to His disciples multiple times over a period of 40 days. He ate with them, taught them, and gave them instructions before ascending to heaven from the Mount of Olives.

beginner.question: Jesus spent 40 days in the desert being tempted by the devil.
beginner.explanation: After His baptism, Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness where He fasted for 40 days and 40 nights. The devil tempted Him three times, but Jesus resisted each temptation by quoting Scripture.

beginner.question: Zacchaeus was a tall man who could easily see over the crowd.
beginner.explanation: Zacchaeus was actually a short man — that's why he had to climb a sycamore-fig tree to see Jesus over the crowd. Despite being a wealthy chief tax collector, his small stature meant he couldn't see past the people.

beginner.question: Judas identified Jesus to the soldiers by kissing Him.
beginner.explanation: Judas had arranged a signal with the soldiers — he would greet the man they should arrest with a kiss. He went to Jesus and said 'Greetings, Rabbi!' and kissed Him, leading to Jesus' arrest.

beginner.question: Pontius Pilate washed his hands to show he didn't want to be responsible for Jesus' death.
beginner.explanation: Pilate took water and washed his hands in front of the crowd, saying 'I am innocent of this man's blood. It is your responsibility!' Despite this gesture, he still allowed Jesus to be crucified.

beginner.question: Peter cut off a soldier's ear when Jesus was being arrested.
beginner.explanation: Peter drew his sword and struck the high priest's servant, cutting off his right ear. Jesus told Peter to put his sword away and then healed the servant's ear — His last miracle before the crucifixion.

beginner.question: 'Jesus wept' is the longest verse in the Bible.
beginner.explanation: 'Jesus wept' (John 11:35) is actually the shortest verse in the Bible, not the longest. It consists of just two words and describes Jesus crying at the tomb of His friend Lazarus.

beginner.question: Jesus healed 10 lepers and all 10 came back to thank Him.
beginner.explanation: Only one of the 10 healed lepers returned to thank Jesus — and he was a Samaritan. Jesus asked 'Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?' This story teaches about gratitude.

beginner.question: Jesus washed His disciples' feet at the Last Supper.
beginner.explanation: Before the meal, Jesus got up, wrapped a towel around His waist, poured water into a basin, and washed His disciples' feet — a task normally done by the lowest servant. He was teaching them about humble service.

beginner.question: A large stone was rolled in front of Jesus' tomb after His burial.
beginner.explanation: Joseph of Arimathea placed Jesus' body in a tomb cut from rock, and a very large stone was rolled across the entrance. The religious leaders also asked Pilate to post guards at the tomb.

beginner.question: Paul was originally called Saul before he became a Christian.
beginner.explanation: Saul was a devout Jew who persecuted Christians before his dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus. After encountering Jesus, he became the apostle Paul — one of the most important figures in the early church.

beginner.question: Saul (Paul) was converted on the road to Bethlehem.
beginner.explanation: Saul's conversion happened on the road to Damascus, not Bethlehem. A bright light from heaven blinded him and Jesus spoke to him, completely transforming his life from persecutor of Christians to apostle.

beginner.question: King Herod wanted to protect baby Jesus.
beginner.explanation: King Herod did not want to protect Jesus — he wanted to kill Him. He felt threatened by the news of a newborn 'King of the Jews' and even ordered the killing of all baby boys in Bethlehem under two years old.

beginner.question: Joseph took Mary and Jesus to Egypt to escape King Herod.
beginner.explanation: An angel warned Joseph in a dream to flee to Egypt because Herod was searching for the child to kill Him. The family stayed in Egypt until King Herod died, then returned to Israel.

beginner.question: The Sermon on the Mount is found in the Old Testament.
beginner.explanation: The Sermon on the Mount is found in the New Testament, in Matthew chapters 5 to 7. It is one of Jesus' most famous teachings and includes the Beatitudes, the Lord's Prayer, and the Golden Rule.

beginner.question: When the devil tempted Jesus in the desert, he told Jesus to turn stones into bread.
beginner.explanation: After 40 days of fasting, Jesus was hungry. The devil said 'If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.' Jesus refused, saying 'Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from God.'

beginner.question: After the feeding of the 5,000, the disciples collected 12 baskets of leftover food.
beginner.explanation: After everyone had eaten their fill from just five loaves and two fish, the disciples picked up 12 full baskets of broken pieces that were left over — showing the miraculous abundance of Jesus' provision.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the good shepherd.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus said 'I am the good shepherd. The good shepherd lays down his life for the sheep.' He was describing how He cares for His followers and would ultimately give His own life for them.

beginner.question: At Jesus' baptism, God's voice from heaven said 'This is my Son, whom I love.'
beginner.explanation: When Jesus came up out of the water after being baptised by John, heaven opened, the Spirit descended like a dove, and God spoke from heaven saying 'This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.'

beginner.question: Lazarus was the brother of Mary and Martha.
beginner.explanation: Lazarus lived in the village of Bethany with his two sisters, Mary and Martha. All three were close friends of Jesus, and Jesus often visited their home.

beginner.question: Jesus told Nicodemus he must be 'born again' to see God's kingdom.
beginner.explanation: Nicodemus, a Pharisee and religious leader, came to Jesus at night. Jesus told him 'Very truly I tell you, no one can see the kingdom of God unless they are born again,' meaning a spiritual rebirth through faith.

beginner.question: The Holy Spirit descended on Jesus like an eagle at His baptism.
beginner.explanation: The Holy Spirit descended on Jesus like a dove, not an eagle. The dove is a symbol of peace and gentleness, and this moment at Jesus' baptism marked the beginning of His public ministry.

beginner.question: On the day of Pentecost, tongues of fire appeared above the disciples' heads.
beginner.explanation: When the Holy Spirit came at Pentecost, what looked like tongues of fire separated and rested on each of the disciples. They were all filled with the Holy Spirit and began speaking in other languages.

beginner.question: Mary visited her relative Elizabeth before Jesus was born.
beginner.explanation: After the angel Gabriel told Mary she would give birth to Jesus, Mary went to visit Elizabeth, who was also miraculously pregnant with John the Baptist. Mary stayed with Elizabeth for about three months.

beginner.question: Elizabeth's baby (John the Baptist) leaped for joy inside her when Mary arrived.
beginner.explanation: When Elizabeth heard Mary's greeting, the baby inside her leaped. Elizabeth was filled with the Holy Spirit and exclaimed 'Blessed are you among women, and blessed is the child you will bear!'

beginner.question: Jesus said 'Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.'
beginner.explanation: This is one of the Beatitudes from the Sermon on the Mount. Jesus was teaching that those who work for peace and reconciliation hold a special place in God's family.

beginner.question: The sky went dark for three hours while Jesus was on the cross.
beginner.explanation: From noon until three in the afternoon, darkness came over the whole land while Jesus hung on the cross. This supernatural darkness lasted three hours, ending when Jesus cried out and gave up His spirit.

beginner.question: Peter, James, and John were Jesus' three closest disciples.
beginner.explanation: Peter, James, and John formed Jesus' inner circle. They were present at key moments others missed — like the Transfiguration (where Jesus' appearance changed on a mountain) and His deepest prayer in Gethsemane.

beginner.question: Mark is the second book of the New Testament.
beginner.explanation: The order of the first four books (the Gospels) is Matthew, Mark, Luke, and John. Mark is the shortest Gospel and is known for its fast-paced, action-oriented telling of Jesus' story.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the vine, you are the branches.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus used this image to teach that His followers must stay connected to Him to bear spiritual fruit. 'If you remain in me and I in you, you will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing.'

beginner.question: In Jesus' parable, the wise man built his house on sand.
beginner.explanation: The wise man built his house on rock, not sand. It was the foolish man who built on sand. When storms came, the house on rock stood firm while the house on sand collapsed.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'Give to Caesar what is Caesar's, and to God what is God's.'
beginner.explanation: When people tried to trap Jesus with a question about paying taxes to the Roman emperor, He asked whose image was on the coin. They said 'Caesar's.' So He gave this brilliant answer about duty to both government and God.

beginner.question: The sign above Jesus' cross read 'King of the Jews.'
beginner.explanation: Pontius Pilate had a sign prepared that read 'JESUS OF NAZARETH, THE KING OF THE JEWS.' It was written in three languages — Aramaic, Latin, and Greek — so everyone could read it.

beginner.question: Roman soldiers put a purple robe on Jesus to mock Him as a king.
beginner.explanation: The soldiers dressed Jesus in a purple (or scarlet) robe — purple being the colour of royalty — to make fun of His claim to be a king. They also pressed a crown of thorns on His head and struck Him.

beginner.question: Joseph of Arimathea provided his own tomb for Jesus' burial.
beginner.explanation: Joseph of Arimathea, a wealthy and respected member of the Jewish council, went to Pilate and asked for Jesus' body. He wrapped it in clean linen and placed it in his own new tomb, carved out of rock.

beginner.question: Jesus spoke to a Samaritan woman at a well and offered her 'living water.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus met a Samaritan woman at Jacob's well and asked her for a drink. He then told her about 'living water' — a spiritual water that would give eternal life. She became one of the first people to spread the news about Jesus.

beginner.question: Five loaves of bread and two fish were used to feed the 5,000.
beginner.explanation: A young boy had five small barley loaves and two small fish. Jesus took them, gave thanks, and miraculously multiplied them to feed over 5,000 men, plus women and children.

beginner.question: The temple curtain was torn from top to bottom when Jesus died.
beginner.explanation: At the exact moment Jesus died, the massive curtain in the Temple — which separated the Holy of Holies from the rest — was torn in two from top to bottom, symbolising that everyone now had direct access to God.

beginner.question: The crowd chose to free Barabbas instead of Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Pilate offered to release one prisoner during Passover. Stirred up by the religious leaders, the crowd chose Barabbas — a criminal — and demanded that Jesus be crucified.

beginner.question: On the cross, Jesus said 'Father, forgive them, for they do not know what they are doing.'
beginner.explanation: Even while suffering on the cross, Jesus prayed for forgiveness for those who were crucifying Him. This extraordinary prayer shows the depth of Jesus' love and mercy.

beginner.question: Thomas declared 'My Lord and my God!' when he saw the risen Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Thomas had refused to believe Jesus was alive until he could see and touch the wounds. When Jesus appeared and showed him, Thomas immediately believed and made this powerful declaration of faith.

beginner.question: Jesus said 'I am the light of the world.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus declared 'I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will never walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.' This is one of seven famous 'I am' statements found in John's Gospel.

beginner.question: At the wedding in Cana, a servant told Jesus the wine had run out.
beginner.explanation: It was Jesus' mother Mary, not a servant, who told Him the wine had run out. Mary then told the servants 'Do whatever he tells you,' and Jesus performed His first miracle by turning water into wine.

beginner.question: Jesus ascended to heaven 40 days after His resurrection.
beginner.explanation: After rising from the dead, Jesus appeared to His disciples over a period of 40 days. On the 40th day, He was taken up into heaven from the Mount of Olives while they watched.

beginner.question: Peter briefly walked on water before becoming afraid and sinking.
beginner.explanation: When Peter saw Jesus walking on water, he asked to come to Him. Jesus said 'Come.' Peter stepped out of the boat and walked on water, but when he saw the wind and waves, he became afraid and began to sink.

beginner.question: Two criminals were crucified alongside Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Two criminals were crucified with Jesus — one on His right and one on His left. One mocked Jesus, but the other asked Jesus to remember him, and Jesus promised him paradise.

beginner.question: One of the criminals on the cross asked Jesus to remember him, and Jesus promised he would be with Him in paradise.
beginner.explanation: The repentant criminal said 'Jesus, remember me when you come into your kingdom.' Jesus replied 'Truly I tell you, today you will be with me in paradise' — one of the most beautiful promises in the Bible.

beginner.question: An angel rolled away the stone from Jesus' tomb on Easter morning.
beginner.explanation: There was a violent earthquake, and an angel of the Lord descended from heaven, rolled back the stone, and sat on it. His appearance was like lightning, and the guards were so afraid they shook and became like dead men.

beginner.question: The word 'Gospel' means 'bad news.'
beginner.explanation: The word 'Gospel' means 'good news,' not bad news. It refers to the good news about Jesus Christ — that God sent His Son to save the world through His life, death, and resurrection.

beginner.question: Friends lowered a paralysed man through a roof to get him to Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Four friends carried a paralysed man to the house where Jesus was teaching, but it was too crowded to get in. So they went up on the roof, made a hole, and lowered him down on his mat right in front of Jesus.

beginner.question: In the parable of the mustard seed, a tiny seed grows into a large plant.
beginner.explanation: Jesus compared the kingdom of God to a mustard seed — one of the smallest seeds — that grows into the largest of garden plants, big enough for birds to rest in its branches.

beginner.question: Jesus' final words on the cross included 'It is finished.'
beginner.explanation: Just before dying, Jesus said 'It is finished.' These words declared that His mission to save humanity through His sacrifice was complete. He had accomplished everything He came to do.

beginner.question: The book of Acts comes right after the four Gospels in the New Testament.
beginner.explanation: The order is: Matthew, Mark, Luke, John, then Acts. Acts picks up where Luke's Gospel ends and tells the story of how the early church grew and spread after Jesus ascended to heaven.

beginner.question: When Jesus was 12 years old, He was found in the Temple talking with teachers.
beginner.explanation: During a visit to Jerusalem for the Passover, the young Jesus stayed behind in the Temple. His parents searched for three days before finding Him sitting among the teachers, listening and asking questions.

beginner.question: The crowd released Jesus and crucified Barabbas.
beginner.explanation: It was the opposite — the crowd released Barabbas (the criminal) and demanded that Jesus be crucified. Pilate gave in to their demands despite finding no fault in Jesus.

beginner.question: Zacchaeus promised to give half of his possessions to the poor after meeting Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Zacchaeus was so changed by his encounter with Jesus that he stood up and said 'Here and now I give half of my possessions to the poor, and if I have cheated anybody, I will pay back four times the amount.'

beginner.question: Jesus said 'You of little faith, why did you doubt?' to Peter when he was sinking in the water.
beginner.explanation: After Peter stepped out of the boat and briefly walked on water, he became afraid of the wind and started sinking. He cried 'Lord, save me!' and Jesus reached out, caught him, and said these words.

beginner.question: Paul travelled to many different countries to tell people about Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Paul made at least three major missionary journeys across the Roman Empire — through modern-day Turkey, Greece, and eventually to Rome — establishing churches and spreading the message of Jesus everywhere he went.

beginner.question: The title 'Christ' means 'The Anointed One' or 'Messiah.'
