beginner.explanation: Elisha told Naaman to wash seven times in the Jordan River. Naaman was initially insulted by the simple instruction, but when he obeyed, his skin was healed completely and became like a child's.

beginner.question: In the Book of Daniel, a young man was thrown into a den of hungry lions because he refused to stop praying to God. Miraculously, the lions did not hurt him. Who was he?
beginner.explanation: Daniel continued praying to God even though a law was made forbidding it. He was thrown into the lions' den, but God sent an angel who shut the lions' mouths and kept Daniel completely safe.

beginner.question: Daniel was thrown into the lions' den because he broke a law. What was the law that Daniel refused to obey?
options: A law against praying to anyone except the king
A law against eating certain foods
A law against leaving his house
beginner.explanation: King Darius was tricked into signing a decree that anyone who prayed to any god or man other than the king would be thrown into the lions' den. Daniel kept praying to God three times a day as always.

beginner.question: After Daniel survived a night in the lions' den, the king came to check on him in the morning. What had God done to protect Daniel?
options: God put the lions to sleep
God sent an angel to shut the lions' mouths
God turned the lions into stone
beginner.explanation: Daniel told the king that God sent an angel who shut the mouths of the lions so they could not hurt him. Daniel was found completely unharmed with not a scratch on him.

beginner.question: In the Book of Jonah, God told a prophet to go preach to the city of Nineveh, but the prophet ran away instead. God sent a huge creature to swallow him. What swallowed Jonah?
beginner.explanation: God sent a great fish to swallow Jonah after he tried to run away from God's command. Jonah spent three days and three nights inside the fish before it spit him out on dry land.

beginner.question: Jonah was swallowed by a great fish. How long did he stay inside the fish before it spit him out?
beginner.explanation: Jonah was inside the great fish for three days and three nights. During that time, he prayed to God from inside the fish. Then God commanded the fish, and it vomited Jonah onto dry land.

beginner.question: Before Jonah was swallowed by the great fish, he was on a ship trying to flee from God. What happened to the sea that put the ship in danger?
beginner.explanation: God sent a violent storm that threatened to break the ship apart. The sailors eventually threw Jonah overboard at his request, and the sea immediately became calm.

beginner.question: Three young men — Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego — were thrown into a blazing furnace because they refused to do something the king commanded. What did they refuse to do?
options: Bow down to a golden statue
beginner.explanation: King Nebuchadnezzar built a giant golden statue and commanded everyone to bow down and worship it. Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego refused because they worshipped only God.

beginner.question: When King Nebuchadnezzar looked into the fiery furnace after throwing in three men, how many figures did he see walking around inside?
beginner.explanation: The king was amazed to see four figures walking unharmed in the fire, though only three had been thrown in. He said the fourth looked like 'a son of the gods,' believed to be an angel sent by God.

beginner.question: When Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego came out of the fiery furnace, what was remarkable about their condition?
options: They were completely unharmed and didn't even smell of smoke
beginner.explanation: The three men walked out of the furnace completely unharmed. Their hair was not singed, their robes were not scorched, and they didn't even smell of smoke. God had miraculously protected them.

beginner.question: At a wedding celebration in the town of Cana, Jesus performed His very first miracle. What did He do?
beginner.explanation: When the wine ran out at the wedding, Jesus' mother Mary told Him about the problem. Jesus had servants fill six stone jars with water and miraculously turned the water into the finest wine.

beginner.question: Jesus turned water into wine at a wedding. In what town did this first miracle take place?
beginner.explanation: The wedding took place in Cana of Galilee. This was Jesus' first miracle, or 'sign,' and it revealed His glory. His disciples believed in Him after seeing it.

beginner.question: Who told Jesus about the wine running out at the wedding in Cana, which led to His first miracle?
beginner.explanation: Jesus' mother Mary noticed the wine had run out and told Jesus. Although He said His time had not yet come, Mary told the servants to do whatever Jesus said, and He performed the miracle.

beginner.question: In one of Jesus' most famous miracles, He fed a huge crowd with just a small amount of food. How many people did He feed with five loaves of bread and two fish?
beginner.explanation: Jesus fed about 5,000 men, plus women and children, with just five loaves of bread and two fish. After everyone ate and was satisfied, the disciples collected twelve baskets of leftovers.

beginner.question: When Jesus miraculously fed the 5,000, where did the initial food come from?
beginner.explanation: A young boy in the crowd had five small barley loaves and two small fish. Andrew brought the boy to Jesus, who then blessed the food and miraculously multiplied it to feed the entire crowd.

beginner.question: After Jesus miraculously multiplied the bread and fish to feed 5,000 people, how many baskets of leftover food were collected?
beginner.explanation: After everyone had eaten their fill, the disciples picked up twelve baskets of broken pieces of bread and fish. The miracle showed that God provides abundantly — even more than needed.

beginner.question: In a stunning miracle, Jesus walked across the surface of a lake to reach His disciples who were in a boat. Which body of water did He walk on?
beginner.explanation: Jesus walked on the Sea of Galilee to reach His disciples during a storm. The disciples were terrified at first, thinking He was a ghost, but Jesus told them not to be afraid.

beginner.question: When Jesus walked on water, one of His disciples asked to come out and walk on the water too. Jesus said 'Come,' and the disciple stepped out of the boat. Who was this disciple?
beginner.explanation: Peter asked Jesus to call him out onto the water. He actually walked on the water toward Jesus, but when he noticed the strong wind, he became afraid and began to sink. Jesus caught him and said, 'You of little faith, why did you doubt?'

beginner.question: When Peter tried walking on water toward Jesus, what happened when he became afraid and took his eyes off Jesus?
options: He turned back to the boat
beginner.explanation: When Peter saw the wind and waves, he became frightened and started to sink. He cried out 'Lord, save me!' and Jesus immediately reached out His hand and caught him.

beginner.question: Jesus and His disciples were in a boat on the Sea of Galilee when a fierce storm arose. The disciples were terrified, but Jesus was sleeping. What did Jesus do to calm the storm?
options: He spoke to the wind and waves and told them to be still
He rowed the boat to shore
He poured oil on the water
beginner.explanation: Jesus woke up and rebuked the wind and said to the waves, 'Quiet! Be still!' Immediately the wind died down and the sea became completely calm. The disciples were amazed at His power over nature.

beginner.question: When the disciples woke Jesus during the storm on the Sea of Galilee, what question did they ask Him?
options: 'Teacher, don't you care if we drown?'
beginner.explanation: The terrified disciples woke Jesus and said, 'Teacher, don't you care if we drown?' Jesus calmed the storm and then asked them why they had so little faith.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a man who had been blind from birth by putting something on his eyes and telling him to wash in the Pool of Siloam. What did Jesus put on the man's eyes?
options: Mud made from spit and dirt
beginner.explanation: Jesus spit on the ground, made mud with the saliva, and put it on the blind man's eyes. He then told the man to wash in the Pool of Siloam. When the man washed, he could see for the first time.

beginner.question: A man named Bartimaeus sat by the road begging because he could not see. When he heard Jesus was passing by, he shouted for help. What did Jesus do for him?
beginner.explanation: Blind Bartimaeus called out to Jesus for mercy. Jesus stopped, called him over, and said, 'Go, your faith has healed you.' Immediately Bartimaeus received his sight and followed Jesus.

beginner.question: Jesus healed ten men suffering from a terrible skin disease called leprosy. How many of them came back to thank Him?
beginner.explanation: Jesus healed all ten lepers, but only one — a Samaritan — returned to thank Him. Jesus asked, 'Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?' only the foreigner returned to give thanks.

beginner.question: In one healing miracle, four friends brought a paralysed man to Jesus, but the house was so crowded they could not get in. How did they get their friend to Jesus?
options: They lowered him through a hole in the roof
beginner.explanation: The four friends climbed onto the roof, made an opening, and lowered their paralysed friend down on his mat right in front of Jesus. Seeing their faith, Jesus healed the man and forgave his sins.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a woman who had been suffering from bleeding for many years. She was healed when she touched part of Jesus' clothing. How long had she been suffering?
beginner.explanation: The woman had been bleeding for twelve years and had spent all her money on doctors without getting better. She believed that if she just touched Jesus' clothes, she would be healed — and she was.

beginner.question: In Luke chapter 13, Jesus healed a woman who had been bent over and crippled for eighteen years. What did she do immediately after being healed?
beginner.explanation: When Jesus laid His hands on her, immediately she straightened up and praised God. The synagogue leader was angry because Jesus healed on the Sabbath, but the people rejoiced.

beginner.question: At the pool of Bethesda in Jerusalem, Jesus healed a man who had been unable to walk for a very long time. How many years had the man been an invalid?
beginner.explanation: The man had been an invalid for thirty-eight years. Jesus simply told him, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk,' and the man was instantly healed and walked for the first time in decades.

beginner.question: One of Jesus' greatest miracles was raising a man from the dead. This man had been dead for four days when Jesus called him out of his tomb. What was his name?
beginner.explanation: Lazarus of Bethany was the brother of Mary and Martha. He had been dead and buried for four days when Jesus stood at the tomb and called out, 'Lazarus, come out!' and Lazarus walked out alive.

beginner.question: Before raising Lazarus from the dead, Jesus stood at the tomb and did something that showed His deep emotion. What did Jesus famously do?
beginner.explanation: 'Jesus wept' is the shortest verse in the Bible. Seeing the grief of Mary and Martha and the mourners, Jesus was deeply moved and cried, showing His compassion before performing the miracle.

beginner.question: Lazarus was the brother of two sisters who were close friends of Jesus. What were the names of Lazarus' two sisters?
beginner.explanation: Mary and Martha of Bethany were Lazarus' sisters and close friends of Jesus. Martha went to meet Jesus when He arrived, while Mary stayed home weeping until Jesus called for her.

beginner.question: When Jesus arrived to raise Lazarus, Martha said she believed Lazarus would rise again on the last day. Jesus responded with a powerful statement about Himself. He said, 'I am the _____ and the life.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus said, 'I am the resurrection and the life. The one who believes in me will live, even though they die.' This was one of the most important declarations Jesus made about His identity.

beginner.question: A synagogue leader named Jairus came to Jesus begging for help because his young daughter was dying. How old was Jairus' daughter?
beginner.explanation: Jairus' daughter was about twelve years old. By the time Jesus arrived, the girl had already died. Jesus took her hand and said, 'Little girl, I say to you, get up!' and she immediately stood up.

beginner.question: When Jesus arrived at Jairus' house, the people told Jairus not to bother Jesus anymore because the girl had died. What did Jesus say to the mourners?
options: 'The child is not dead but asleep'
beginner.explanation: Jesus told the weeping crowd, 'The child is not dead but asleep.' The people laughed at Him, but Jesus took the girl's hand and raised her back to life, amazing everyone present.

beginner.question: After Jesus' resurrection, His disciples went fishing on the Sea of Galilee but caught nothing all night. Jesus appeared on the shore and told them where to cast their net. What happened?
options: They caught a huge number of fish
The fish jumped into the boat
beginner.explanation: When the disciples cast their net on the right side of the boat as Jesus instructed, they caught so many fish — 153 large ones — that they could not haul the net back into the boat.

beginner.question: In one unusual miracle, Jesus told Peter to go fishing and look inside the first fish he caught. What did Peter find inside the fish's mouth?
beginner.explanation: Jesus told Peter to cast a hook into the sea and open the mouth of the first fish he caught. Inside was a coin — exactly enough to pay the temple tax for both Jesus and Peter.

beginner.question: Jesus took three of His disciples up a high mountain, and His appearance was miraculously transformed — His face shone like the sun and His clothes became dazzling white. What is this event called?
beginner.explanation: The Transfiguration was when Jesus' appearance was gloriously changed on the mountaintop. Moses and Elijah appeared and talked with Him, and a voice from a cloud said, 'This is my Son, whom I love.'

beginner.question: During the Transfiguration on the mountaintop, two figures from the Old Testament appeared and talked with Jesus. Who were they?
beginner.explanation: Moses and Elijah appeared in glorious splendour and spoke with Jesus about what He would accomplish in Jerusalem. Moses represented the Law and Elijah represented the Prophets.

beginner.question: Which three disciples did Jesus take with Him up the mountain for the Transfiguration?
beginner.explanation: Jesus took Peter, James, and John — His three closest disciples — up the mountain. These same three were often present for the most significant moments in Jesus' ministry.

beginner.question: The greatest miracle in the New Testament is the resurrection of Jesus from the dead. On which day of the week did the women discover the empty tomb?
beginner.explanation: Jesus was crucified on Friday and rose from the dead on Sunday, the first day of the week. Women went to the tomb early Sunday morning and found the stone rolled away and the tomb empty.

beginner.question: Jesus was buried in a tomb carved from rock, and a large stone was rolled across the entrance. After His resurrection, what did visitors find when they arrived at the tomb?
options: The stone was still in place
The stone had been rolled away and the tomb was empty
beginner.explanation: When the women arrived at the tomb early Sunday morning, they found that the large stone had been rolled away. An angel told them that Jesus had risen from the dead, just as He had promised.

beginner.question: How many days after His crucifixion did Jesus rise from the dead?
beginner.explanation: Jesus rose from the dead on the third day, just as He had predicted. He was crucified on Friday, lay in the tomb on Saturday, and rose on Sunday morning.

beginner.question: Who was the first person to see Jesus alive after His resurrection, according to the Gospel of John?
beginner.explanation: Mary Magdalene was the first person to see the risen Jesus. She initially mistook Him for the gardener, but when Jesus said her name, she recognised Him and was overjoyed.

beginner.question: One of Jesus' disciples refused to believe in the resurrection until he could personally see and touch Jesus' wounds. Which disciple is famous for this doubt?
beginner.explanation: Thomas said he would not believe unless he could see the nail marks in Jesus' hands and put his hand into Jesus' side. When Jesus appeared and invited him to touch His wounds, Thomas believed and said, 'My Lord and my God!'

beginner.question: After His resurrection, Jesus appeared to His disciples many times over a period of days before ascending to heaven. How many days was this period?
beginner.explanation: Jesus appeared to His followers over a period of forty days after His resurrection, giving many convincing proofs that He was alive and teaching them about the kingdom of God.

beginner.question: At the event of Pentecost, after Jesus ascended to heaven, the Holy Spirit came upon the disciples in a dramatic way. What appeared above each disciple's head?
beginner.explanation: On the day of Pentecost, the Holy Spirit came with a sound like a mighty wind, and what appeared to be tongues of fire rested on each of the disciples. They began speaking in other languages.

beginner.question: At Pentecost, when the Holy Spirit came upon the disciples, they received a miraculous ability. What could they suddenly do?
options: Speak in other languages they had never learned
beginner.explanation: The disciples began speaking in various languages they had never learned. Visitors from many countries in Jerusalem were amazed to hear the message of God in their own native languages.

beginner.question: A man named Saul was travelling to the city of Damascus to arrest Christians when Jesus appeared to him in a blinding light. What later became Saul's more well-known name?
beginner.explanation: Saul, who had been persecuting Christians, became the apostle Paul after his dramatic conversion on the road to Damascus. He went on to become one of the most important figures in spreading Christianity.

beginner.question: When Jesus appeared to Saul (Paul) on the road to Damascus, a bright light from heaven flashed around him. What happened to Saul after this encounter?
options: He became blind for three days
He fell into a deep sleep
beginner.explanation: Saul was blinded by the light and could not see for three days. He was led by hand to Damascus, where a disciple named Ananias prayed for him, and his sight was restored.

beginner.question: The apostle Peter was imprisoned by King Herod but was miraculously freed during the night. Who appeared in his prison cell to rescue him?
beginner.explanation: An angel of the Lord appeared in Peter's cell, and a light shone. The angel struck Peter on the side to wake him up, and his chains fell off. The angel led him past the guards and out through the iron gate.

beginner.question: When the angel freed Peter from prison, what happened to the chains on Peter's wrists?
options: The chains fell off by themselves
The angel cut them with a sword
beginner.explanation: When the angel told Peter to get up, the chains simply fell off his wrists. Peter followed the angel past the guards and through the iron gate, which opened by itself.

beginner.question: In the Old Testament, God sent meat to the hungry Israelites in the desert in the form of a bird. What kind of bird did God send?
beginner.explanation: God sent quail in the evening and manna in the morning to feed the Israelites in the wilderness. The quail came in such large numbers that they covered the entire camp.

beginner.question: Moses performed miracles before Pharaoh to convince him to free the Israelites. In the first sign, Moses threw his staff on the ground. What did it turn into?
beginner.explanation: When Moses threw his staff on the ground before Pharaoh, it turned into a snake. Pharaoh's magicians did the same trick, but Moses' snake swallowed up their snakes.

beginner.question: During the plague of hail in Egypt, what unusual feature made the hailstorm miraculous beyond just being a storm?
options: The hailstones were made of gold
Fire flashed along the ground mixed with the hail
The hail only fell at night
beginner.explanation: The hailstorm was unlike any natural storm — there was thunder and hail, and lightning (fire) flashed back and forth along the ground. It was the worst hailstorm in all of Egypt's history.

beginner.question: When the Israelites crossed the Red Sea on dry ground, what was on their left and right sides?
beginner.explanation: God divided the sea and the water formed walls on the left and right of the Israelites as they walked through on completely dry ground. It was a corridor of dry land through the sea.

beginner.question: In the Book of Joshua, God miraculously stopped the sun in the sky so the Israelites could win a battle. Who prayed for the sun to stand still?
beginner.explanation: Joshua commanded, 'Sun, stand still over Gibeon.' God answered his prayer and the sun stopped in the sky for about a full day, giving the Israelites enough daylight to defeat their enemies.

beginner.question: The prophet Elisha performed a miracle to help a woman from Shunem. He raised her son back to life. What had happened to the boy?
options: He had a severe headache and died
He was attacked by a bear
beginner.explanation: The Shunammite woman's son complained of a bad headache while in the fields. He was brought to his mother but died on her lap. Elisha prayed and lay on the child, and the boy came back to life.

beginner.question: The prophet Elisha performed a miracle involving poisoned stew. When the men cried out that there was death in the pot, what did Elisha add to make it safe?
beginner.explanation: When the prophets discovered the stew was poisonous from wild gourds, Elisha told them to bring flour. He threw flour into the pot, and the stew became perfectly safe to eat.

beginner.question: Elisha performed a miracle similar to Jesus feeding the 5,000. He fed 100 men with just twenty loaves of barley bread. What happened after everyone ate?
beginner.explanation: Just as God promised through Elisha, the hundred men ate and there was bread left over, exactly as the word of the Lord had said. God miraculously multiplied the small amount of food.

beginner.question: In one miracle, Elisha made a borrowed iron axe head float on water after it fell into the Jordan River. What did he throw into the water to make it float?
beginner.explanation: When a man lost a borrowed axe head in the Jordan River, Elisha cut a stick and threw it into the water at the spot where the iron had sunk. The heavy iron axe head floated to the surface.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a man who had been deaf and could barely speak. Jesus put His fingers in the man's ears, spit and touched his tongue, then said one word. What was the word?
beginner.explanation: Jesus looked up to heaven, sighed, and said 'Ephphatha!' which means 'Be opened!' Immediately the man's ears were opened, his tongue was loosened, and he began to speak clearly.

beginner.question: In Luke chapter 7, Jesus encountered a funeral procession and raised a young man from the dead. This man was the only son of a widow. In which town did this happen?
beginner.explanation: At the town of Nain, Jesus saw a widow's only son being carried out for burial. Moved with compassion, He touched the coffin and said, 'Young man, I say to you, get up!' The dead man sat up and began to talk.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a man with a withered hand on the Sabbath, which upset the religious leaders. Where did this healing take place?
beginner.explanation: Jesus healed the man with the shrivelled hand in the synagogue on the Sabbath day. He asked the Pharisees whether it was lawful to do good on the Sabbath, then told the man to stretch out his hand, and it was restored.

beginner.question: Jesus cast out many demons during His ministry. In one famous incident, He sent a large number of demons out of a man and into a herd of animals. What animals did the demons enter?
beginner.explanation: The demons, who called themselves 'Legion' because there were so many, begged Jesus to send them into a herd of pigs. When Jesus allowed it, the entire herd rushed down a steep bank into the sea and drowned.

beginner.question: In the Old Testament, when the Israelites wandered in the desert for forty years, God provided for them miraculously. What happened to their clothing and sandals during those forty years?
beginner.explanation: Moses told the Israelites that during their forty years in the wilderness, their clothes did not wear out and their sandals did not fall apart. God miraculously preserved everything they needed.

beginner.question: During the Exodus, God guided the Israelites through the wilderness using two miraculous signs — one for day and one for night. What were they?
options: A pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night
beginner.explanation: God led the Israelites with a pillar of cloud during the day to guide their way, and a pillar of fire at night to give them light. These signs showed God's constant presence and guidance.

beginner.question: When Aaron's staff was placed overnight in the Tabernacle along with the staffs of other tribal leaders, something miraculous happened to it. What did Aaron's staff do?
options: It sprouted buds, blossomed, and produced almonds
beginner.explanation: God caused Aaron's staff to sprout, bud, blossom, and produce ripe almonds overnight. This miracle confirmed that God had chosen Aaron and the tribe of Levi for the priesthood.

beginner.question: In the story of Gideon, God gave him a miraculous sign using a fleece (a piece of wool). Gideon asked that the fleece would be wet with dew while the ground around it stayed dry. What happened?
options: God did exactly what Gideon asked
beginner.explanation: God did exactly as Gideon asked — the fleece was soaking wet with dew while the ground around it was completely dry. Gideon then asked for the reverse sign, and God did that too, confirming His promise.

beginner.question: Jesus performed a miracle at a wedding because the hosts ran out of something. What had they run out of?
beginner.explanation: At the wedding in Cana, the wine ran out, which would have been a great embarrassment. Jesus' mother told Him about the problem, and He turned water into wine — the finest wine at the feast.

beginner.question: How many stone water jars did Jesus use when He turned water into wine at the wedding in Cana?
beginner.explanation: There were six stone water jars, each holding twenty to thirty gallons. Jesus told the servants to fill them with water, and when they drew some out, it had become excellent wine.

beginner.question: Jesus once healed a Roman centurion's servant without even going to the centurion's house. What did Jesus say amazed Him about this centurion?
beginner.explanation: The centurion said he wasn't worthy to have Jesus enter his home, and believed Jesus could heal his servant with just a word. Jesus said He had not found such great faith even in Israel.

beginner.question: When Jesus fed the 4,000 (a separate miracle from the feeding of the 5,000), how many baskets of leftover food were collected?
beginner.explanation: After feeding 4,000 men plus women and children with seven loaves and a few small fish, the disciples collected seven baskets of broken pieces left over. This was a second mass feeding miracle.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a man who was born blind. When asked who sinned to cause the blindness, what did Jesus say?
options: 'Neither — this happened so that God's work might be displayed'
beginner.explanation: Jesus rejected the idea that the man's blindness was caused by anyone's sin. He said it happened so that the works of God could be displayed in his life, and then He healed the man.

beginner.question: In the Book of Numbers, the Israelites were bitten by poisonous snakes in the desert. God told Moses to make a snake and put it on a pole. What happened when people looked at it?
beginner.explanation: God told Moses to make a bronze snake and put it on a pole. Anyone who was bitten by a snake could look at the bronze snake and be healed. This later became a symbol of salvation.

beginner.question: The prophet Elijah was fed by ravens during a drought. Where did God tell him to stay while the ravens brought him food?
beginner.explanation: God told Elijah to hide by the Kerith Ravine, east of the Jordan. Ravens brought him bread and meat in the morning and evening, and he drank water from the brook until it dried up.

beginner.question: The prophet Elijah stayed with a poor widow during a famine. God made her jar of flour and jug of oil last miraculously long. How long did the food supply last?
beginner.explanation: God made the widow's jar of flour and jug of oil never run out, just as Elijah had promised. The food lasted throughout the entire drought until rain came again to the land.

beginner.question: Elijah also raised a boy from the dead. This boy was the son of the widow of Zarephath who had been feeding Elijah. How did Elijah bring the boy back to life?
options: He stretched himself over the boy three times and prayed
He called out the boy's name
beginner.explanation: Elijah carried the boy to his room, stretched himself over the child three times, and cried out to the Lord. God heard his prayer, and the boy's life returned to him.

beginner.question: When the prophet Elisha crossed the Jordan River, he struck the water with something, and the water divided so he could walk through on dry ground. What did he use?
beginner.explanation: Elisha picked up the cloak that had fallen from Elijah. He struck the water of the Jordan with it and said, 'Where now is the Lord, the God of Elijah?' The water divided, and Elisha crossed over.

beginner.question: After Jesus ascended to heaven, the apostle Peter healed a man who had been lame from birth. Where was this lame man sitting when Peter healed him?
options: At the gate of the temple called Beautiful
beginner.explanation: The lame man sat at the temple gate called Beautiful, begging for money. Peter said, 'Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ, walk.' The man jumped up and walked.

beginner.question: When Peter and John healed the lame man at the temple gate, what did Peter say he did not have?
beginner.explanation: Peter famously said, 'Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.' The man was healed instantly and began leaping and praising God.

beginner.question: In the Old Testament, God created the first woman, Eve, from what part of Adam's body?
beginner.explanation: God caused Adam to fall into a deep sleep, took one of his ribs, and fashioned it into Eve. When Adam saw her, he said she was bone of his bone and flesh of his flesh.

beginner.question: In the story of Noah's ark, how long did it rain during the great flood?
beginner.explanation: It rained for forty days and forty nights during the great flood. The floodwaters rose and covered even the highest mountains, and every living thing on earth was destroyed except those on the ark.

beginner.question: When the Israelites were dying of thirst at Rephidim in the desert, God told Moses to strike a rock at a specific place. What mountain was this rock located at?
beginner.explanation: God told Moses to strike the rock at Horeb, and water came flowing out. The place was named Massah and Meribah because the Israelites quarrelled and tested the Lord there.

beginner.question: The prophet Balaam was riding his donkey when the animal suddenly stopped and refused to move. The Bible says the donkey could see something Balaam could not. What was it?
options: An angel with a drawn sword
beginner.explanation: The donkey saw an angel of the Lord standing in the road with a drawn sword. The donkey tried to avoid the angel three times. Then God opened Balaam's eyes so he could also see the angel.

beginner.question: In one of the strangest miracles in the Bible, Balaam's donkey did something no animal had ever done before. What miraculous thing did the donkey do?
beginner.explanation: God opened the donkey's mouth and it spoke to Balaam in human language, complaining about being beaten. This is one of only two instances in the Bible where an animal speaks.

beginner.question: How many fish did Peter and the disciples catch in the miraculous catch of fish after Jesus' resurrection, when Jesus told them to cast their net on the right side of the boat?
beginner.explanation: The disciples caught exactly 153 large fish. Despite the huge number, the net was not torn. This miracle helped the disciples recognise that it was the Lord standing on the shore.

beginner.question: During Jesus' crucifixion, several miraculous events occurred. What happened to the curtain in the temple at the moment Jesus died?
options: It was torn in two from top to bottom
beginner.explanation: At the moment Jesus died, the thick curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom. This curtain had separated the Holy of Holies from the rest of the temple, symbolising that access to God was now open to all.

beginner.question: What other miraculous event happened at the moment of Jesus' death besides the temple curtain tearing?
beginner.explanation: When Jesus died, the earth shook and rocks split apart. The earthquake was so dramatic that the Roman centurion guarding Jesus exclaimed, 'Surely He was the Son of God!'

beginner.question: When baby Jesus was born, a miraculous sign appeared in the sky that guided wise men from the East to find Him. What was this sign?
beginner.explanation: A special star appeared in the sky when Jesus was born. Wise men (Magi) from the East saw this star and followed it all the way to Bethlehem, where they found the child Jesus and worshipped Him.

beginner.question: Before Jesus was born, an angel appeared to a young woman to tell her she would give birth to the Son of God. What was this young woman's name?
beginner.explanation: The angel Gabriel appeared to Mary (also called the Virgin Mary) in Nazareth and told her she would conceive by the Holy Spirit and give birth to Jesus, the Son of the Most High.

beginner.question: Jesus ascended to heaven in front of His disciples after His resurrection. What happened as He went up?
options: A cloud hid Him from their sight
beginner.explanation: As the disciples watched, Jesus was taken up before their very eyes, and a cloud hid Him from their sight. Two angels then appeared and told them Jesus would return in the same way.

beginner.question: In the Old Testament, the walls of Jericho fell down after the Israelites marched around the city. What instruments did the priests carry and blow?
beginner.explanation: Seven priests carried trumpets made of ram's horns (shofars) and blew them as they marched around Jericho. On the seventh day, after the final blast, the people shouted and the walls collapsed.

beginner.question: When Moses led the Israelites out of Egypt, what happened to the Egyptian army that chased them into the parted Red Sea?
options: The waters closed over them and they drowned
They got lost in the desert
beginner.explanation: After the Israelites crossed safely, Moses stretched his hand over the sea again. The walls of water collapsed on the Egyptian army — their chariots, horses, and soldiers were all swept away and drowned.

beginner.question: When the Israelites followed a pillar of cloud through the wilderness, what happened to the cloud at night?
options: It became a pillar of fire
beginner.explanation: By day the Lord guided them with a pillar of cloud, and by night the same pillar became fire to give them light. This way they could travel day or night, always guided by God's presence.

beginner.question: On which day of creation did God create humans, according to Genesis?
beginner.explanation: God created human beings on the sixth day of creation, after making the animals. He created man in His own image, male and female, and gave them dominion over all living creatures.

beginner.question: In the creation account, God created the sun, moon, and stars on which day?
beginner.explanation: On the fourth day of creation, God made the sun to govern the day, the moon to govern the night, and the stars. He set them in the sky to give light on the earth and mark seasons and days.

beginner.question: What happened to Jesus at His baptism in the Jordan River that was miraculous?
options: The sky opened, the Spirit descended like a dove, and God's voice spoke
Fish jumped out of the water
beginner.explanation: When Jesus was baptised, heaven opened and the Holy Spirit descended on Him in the form of a dove. A voice from heaven said, 'This is my Son, whom I love, with whom I am well pleased.'

beginner.question: After Jesus was tempted in the desert for forty days, who came and ministered to Him?
beginner.explanation: After Jesus resisted all of Satan's temptations in the wilderness, the devil left Him, and angels came and attended to Him, providing comfort and care after His long ordeal.

beginner.question: Paul and Silas were imprisoned in Philippi. While they were praying and singing hymns at midnight, what miraculous event freed them?
options: A flood washed away the prison
An earthquake shook the prison doors open
beginner.explanation: A violent earthquake shook the foundations of the prison. All the doors flew open and everyone's chains came loose. The jailer was about to kill himself but Paul stopped him, and the jailer became a believer.

beginner.question: The apostle Paul was bitten by a venomous snake on the island of Malta. What happened to him after the bite?
options: He suffered no harm at all
beginner.explanation: Paul shook the snake off into the fire and suffered no ill effects. The islanders expected him to swell up and die, but when nothing happened, they believed God was with him.

beginner.question: In the Book of Acts, a disciple named Tabitha (also called Dorcas) died. Which apostle raised her back to life?
beginner.explanation: Peter was called to the room where Tabitha lay dead. He sent everyone out, knelt down, and prayed. Then he said, 'Tabitha, get up.' She opened her eyes, and Peter helped her to her feet.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a woman who had been bent over and unable to stand up straight for how many years?
beginner.explanation: The woman had been crippled by an evil spirit for eighteen years and could not straighten up at all. Jesus called her forward, laid His hands on her, and immediately she stood up straight and praised God.

beginner.question: When Jesus healed the man at the pool of Bethesda, what did He tell the healed man to do?
options: Pick up his mat and walk
beginner.explanation: Jesus said to the man, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk.' At once the man was cured — he picked up his mat and walked. This happened on the Sabbath, which angered the Jewish leaders.

beginner.question: When Elisha's servant was afraid because an enemy army surrounded them, Elisha prayed that God would open the servant's eyes. What did the servant then see?
options: Hills full of horses and chariots of fire
beginner.explanation: When God opened the servant's eyes, he saw the hills full of horses and chariots of fire all around Elisha. God's heavenly army far outnumbered the enemy forces, protecting the prophet.

beginner.question: When Jesus cursed a fig tree because it had no fruit, what happened to the tree?
beginner.explanation: Jesus cursed the fig tree, and by the next morning it had withered from the roots. The disciples were amazed. Jesus used the moment to teach about the power of faith and prayer.

beginner.question: In the Book of Acts, Philip the evangelist was miraculously transported from one place to another after baptising an Ethiopian official. This is sometimes called 'Philip's transport.' What happened to him?
options: He was carried by an angel
The Spirit of the Lord suddenly took him away to another place
beginner.explanation: After Philip baptised the Ethiopian eunuch, the Spirit of the Lord suddenly took Philip away. The Ethiopian did not see him again, and Philip appeared at the town of Azotus and continued preaching.

beginner.question: When Jesus healed the ten lepers, what did He tell them to do?
options: Go wash in the Jordan River
Go show themselves to the priests
beginner.explanation: Jesus told the ten lepers to go show themselves to the priests, as the Law of Moses required for someone healed of leprosy. As they went, they were cleansed — but only one returned to thank Jesus.

beginner.question: In the Old Testament, what miracle did God perform at the Tower of Babel when people tried to build a tower to reach heaven?
options: He destroyed the tower with lightning
He confused their languages so they could not understand each other
beginner.explanation: God confused the language of the people at Babel so they could no longer understand each other. Unable to communicate, they stopped building the tower and scattered across the earth.

beginner.question: When the Jordan River was in flood, the Israelites needed to cross it to enter the Promised Land. What happened when the priests carrying the Ark of the Covenant stepped into the water?
options: The water stopped flowing and piled up in a heap
beginner.explanation: As soon as the priests' feet touched the water, the Jordan stopped flowing. The water piled up in a heap far upstream, and the people crossed on dry ground — similar to the Red Sea crossing.

beginner.question: Jesus performed a miracle for Simon Peter at the very beginning of their relationship. Jesus told Peter where to cast his fishing nets, and Peter caught so many fish that what happened?
options: The boat flew into the air
beginner.explanation: Peter had fished all night and caught nothing, but when Jesus told him to cast his nets in deep water, he caught so many fish that the nets began to break. Peter had to call another boat to help.

beginner.question: In the New Testament, a young man named Eutychus fell asleep during one of Paul's long sermons and fell out of a window. What miracle did Paul perform?
options: He raised Eutychus from the dead
beginner.explanation: Eutychus fell from the third floor and was picked up dead. Paul went down, threw himself on the young man, and embraced him. Paul said, 'Don't be alarmed — he's alive!' and the boy was restored to life.

beginner.question: God parted the Red Sea so Moses and the Israelites could escape from the Egyptian army.
beginner.explanation: God told Moses to stretch his hand over the sea. The waters divided, and the Israelites walked through on dry ground with walls of water on each side. The Egyptian army drowned when the waters returned.

beginner.question: According to the Book of Genesis, God created the entire world in ten days.
beginner.explanation: The Bible says God created the world in six days and rested on the seventh day, not ten days. The seven-day creation account is one of the most well-known stories in the Bible.

beginner.question: In the creation story, God made the first man, Adam, from clay mixed with water.
beginner.explanation: The Bible says God formed Adam from the dust of the ground — not clay mixed with water. God then breathed the breath of life into his nostrils, and Adam became a living being.

beginner.question: God told Noah to build a large boat called an ark to save his family and the animals from a great flood.
beginner.explanation: God warned Noah that He would flood the earth because of human wickedness. Noah was instructed to build an ark and bring two of every kind of animal aboard to preserve life through the flood.

beginner.question: After the great flood, God placed a star in the sky as a promise never to flood the earth again.
beginner.explanation: God placed a rainbow — not a star — in the sky as a sign of His covenant with Noah. The rainbow was God's promise that He would never again destroy all life on earth with a flood.

beginner.question: Moses saw a bush that was on fire but was not burning up, and God spoke to him from the bush.
beginner.explanation: While tending sheep near Mount Horeb, Moses noticed a bush that was burning but not being consumed by the flames. God spoke to him from the bush and called him to lead the Israelites out of Egypt.

beginner.question: God sent seven plagues upon Egypt before Pharaoh agreed to let the Israelites go.
beginner.explanation: God sent ten plagues upon Egypt, not seven. The ten plagues included water turning to blood, frogs, gnats, flies, livestock disease, boils, hail, locusts, darkness, and the death of the firstborn.

beginner.question: The first plague of Egypt turned the water of the Nile River into blood.
beginner.explanation: God turned the water of the Nile into blood as the first of the ten plagues. All the fish died, the river smelled terrible, and the Egyptians could not drink from it.

beginner.question: The tenth and final plague of Egypt killed every firstborn son in the land.
beginner.explanation: In the most devastating plague, every firstborn son in Egypt died, from the son of Pharaoh to the sons of prisoners. The Israelites were protected by putting lamb's blood on their doorposts.

beginner.question: The Israelites painted their doors with olive oil to protect their firstborn from the last plague in Egypt.
beginner.explanation: The Israelites put lamb's blood — not olive oil — on their doorposts. When the angel of death saw the blood, it 'passed over' that house. This event is why the holiday is called Passover.

beginner.question: When the Israelites crossed through the parted Red Sea, they walked on dry ground with walls of water on both sides.
beginner.explanation: God divided the waters of the Red Sea, and the Israelites walked through on completely dry ground. The water stood up like walls on their left and their right as they crossed safely.

beginner.question: God sent food called manna from heaven to feed the Israelites as they wandered in the desert.
beginner.explanation: Manna appeared on the ground each morning like frost. The Israelites collected it daily and used it to make bread. God provided this miraculous food for the entire forty years they were in the wilderness.

beginner.question: Moses struck a rock with his staff in the desert, and honey flowed out for the Israelites to eat.
beginner.explanation: When Moses struck the rock, water — not honey — gushed out. The Israelites were dying of thirst in the desert, and God miraculously provided water from the rock to quench their thirst.

beginner.question: The walls of Jericho fell down after the Israelites marched around the city for seven days.
beginner.explanation: God told Joshua and the Israelites to march around Jericho once a day for six days and seven times on the seventh day. When they blew their trumpets and shouted, the walls collapsed miraculously.

beginner.question: Moses was the leader of the Israelites when the walls of Jericho fell down.
beginner.explanation: Joshua — not Moses — was the leader when Jericho's walls fell. Moses had already died before the Israelites entered the Promised Land. Joshua succeeded Moses and led the people to victory at Jericho.

beginner.question: Samson's miraculous strength came from a special bracelet he wore on his wrist.
beginner.explanation: Samson's strength was connected to his uncut hair as part of his Nazirite vow to God — not a bracelet. When Delilah had his hair cut while he slept, his strength left him.

beginner.question: Samson killed a lion with his bare hands through the power God gave him.
beginner.explanation: The Spirit of the Lord came upon Samson with great power, and he tore a young lion apart with his bare hands as easily as if it were a young goat. This demonstrated his God-given supernatural strength.

beginner.question: Delilah was the woman who discovered the secret of Samson's strength and betrayed him to the Philistines.
beginner.explanation: Delilah was bribed by the Philistines to learn the secret of Samson's strength. After much persistence, Samson told her his hair had never been cut. She had it shaved while he slept, and his strength departed.

beginner.question: The prophet Elijah defeated 450 prophets of the false god Baal in a contest on Mount Carmel when God sent fire from heaven.
beginner.explanation: Elijah challenged the prophets of Baal to see whose god would send fire to consume an offering. Baal's prophets prayed all day with no response, but when Elijah prayed, God sent fire that consumed his offering completely.

beginner.question: Before God sent fire from heaven on Mount Carmel, Elijah poured oil over his offering to make it easier to burn.
beginner.explanation: Elijah poured water over his offering — not oil — three times, filling the trench around the altar. He did this to make the miracle more impressive, showing that only God's power could ignite a waterlogged sacrifice.

beginner.question: The prophet Elijah was taken up to heaven in a chariot of fire and a whirlwind.
beginner.explanation: Elijah was one of only two people in the Bible who never died. A chariot of fire with horses of fire appeared, and Elijah was taken up to heaven in a whirlwind while his student Elisha watched.

beginner.question: The prophet Isaiah was the one who watched Elijah being taken up to heaven in a chariot of fire.
beginner.explanation: It was Elisha — not Isaiah — who watched Elijah ascend to heaven. Elisha was Elijah's faithful student and successor. After watching the departure, he picked up Elijah's fallen cloak.

beginner.question: The prophet Elisha healed the army commander Naaman of leprosy by telling him to wash seven times in the Nile River.
beginner.explanation: Elisha told Naaman to wash in the Jordan River — not the Nile. Naaman was initially angry at the simple instruction but obeyed, and after washing seven times, his skin was completely healed.

beginner.question: Daniel was thrown into a den of lions because he refused to stop praying to God.
beginner.explanation: A decree was passed forbidding prayer to anyone except the king. Daniel continued praying to God three times daily as always. He was thrown into the lions' den, but God sent an angel to shut the lions' mouths.

beginner.question: God sent an angel to shut the lions' mouths so Daniel was not harmed in the lions' den.
beginner.explanation: Daniel told the king that God sent His angel to shut the lions' mouths. When Daniel was pulled out in the morning, not a single scratch was found on him because he had trusted in God.

beginner.question: Jonah was swallowed by a great fish and survived inside it for seven days and seven nights.
beginner.explanation: Jonah was inside the great fish for three days and three nights — not seven. He prayed to God from inside the fish, and God commanded the fish to spit Jonah out onto dry land.

beginner.question: God sent a great fish to swallow Jonah after Jonah tried to run away from God's command to go to Nineveh.
beginner.explanation: God told Jonah to preach to the city of Nineveh, but Jonah boarded a ship going the opposite direction. God sent a storm, and after Jonah was thrown overboard, God provided a great fish to swallow him.

beginner.question: Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego were thrown into a lions' den for refusing to bow down to a golden statue.
beginner.explanation: They were thrown into a fiery furnace — not a lions' den. It was Daniel who was thrown into the lions' den in a separate story. Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego survived the blazing furnace unharmed.

beginner.question: When the king looked into the fiery furnace, he saw only three men walking around — the same three that were thrown in.
beginner.explanation: The king saw four figures walking in the fire, not three. Three had been thrown in, but a mysterious fourth figure was with them, whom the king described as looking like 'a son of the gods' — believed to be an angel.

beginner.question: When Shadrach, Meshach, and Abednego came out of the fiery furnace, their clothes were burned but they were alive.
beginner.explanation: They came out completely unharmed — not even their clothes were burned, their hair was not singed, and they did not even smell of smoke. God miraculously protected them from all effects of the fire.

beginner.question: Jesus' first miracle was turning water into wine at a wedding in the town of Cana.
beginner.explanation: At a wedding in Cana of Galilee, the wine ran out. Jesus told the servants to fill six stone jars with water, and when they drew some out, it had become excellent wine. This was His first miracle.

beginner.question: Jesus' first miracle was healing a blind man in Jerusalem.
beginner.explanation: Jesus' first miracle was turning water into wine at a wedding in Cana of Galilee — not healing a blind man. The Gospel of John specifically identifies this as the first of Jesus' miraculous signs.

beginner.question: Jesus fed about 5,000 people using just five loaves of bread and two fish.
beginner.explanation: A boy had five small barley loaves and two fish. Jesus blessed the food and miraculously multiplied it to feed about 5,000 men, plus women and children. Twelve baskets of leftovers were collected.

beginner.question: After Jesus fed the 5,000, the disciples collected seven baskets of leftover food.
beginner.explanation: After the feeding of the 5,000, the disciples collected twelve baskets of leftovers — not seven. It was after the separate miracle of feeding the 4,000 that seven baskets were collected.

beginner.question: Jesus walked on the water of the Sea of Galilee to reach His disciples who were in a boat.
beginner.explanation: Jesus walked on the water during a storm on the Sea of Galilee. The disciples were terrified and thought He was a ghost, but Jesus told them to take courage and not be afraid.

beginner.question: The apostle John stepped out of the boat and walked on water toward Jesus.
beginner.explanation: It was Peter — not John — who stepped out of the boat and walked on the water toward Jesus. Peter began to sink when he took his eyes off Jesus and became afraid of the wind and waves.

beginner.question: Peter began to sink when he was walking on water because he became afraid of the wind and waves.
beginner.explanation: Peter was walking on the water toward Jesus, but when he saw the wind and waves, he became frightened and began to sink. He cried out, 'Lord, save me!' and Jesus immediately reached out and caught him.

beginner.question: Jesus calmed a fierce storm on the Sea of Galilee by speaking to the wind and waves and telling them to be still.
beginner.explanation: While the disciples were panicking during a storm, Jesus woke up and said, 'Quiet! Be still!' The wind stopped and the sea became completely calm. The disciples were amazed at His power over nature.

beginner.question: Jesus was awake and praying on the boat when the great storm arose on the Sea of Galilee.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was sleeping on a cushion in the back of the boat when the storm arose — not awake and praying. The frightened disciples woke Him, and He then calmed the storm with a word.

beginner.question: Jesus raised a man named Lazarus from the dead after Lazarus had been in the tomb for four days.
beginner.explanation: Lazarus of Bethany had been dead for four days when Jesus arrived. Jesus stood at the tomb and called out, 'Lazarus, come out!' and Lazarus walked out alive, still wrapped in burial cloths.

beginner.question: 'Jesus wept' is the shortest verse in the Bible, and it occurred when Jesus mourned at the tomb of Lazarus.
beginner.explanation: John 11:35 simply says 'Jesus wept.' Seeing the grief of Mary, Martha, and the mourners, Jesus was deeply moved and cried, showing His compassion before raising Lazarus from the dead.

beginner.question: Lazarus was the brother of Mary and Martha, and all three were close friends of Jesus.
beginner.explanation: Lazarus, Mary, and Martha lived in Bethany and were dear friends of Jesus. When Jesus heard Lazarus was sick, the sisters sent word to Him, saying, 'Lord, the one you love is sick.'

beginner.question: Jesus raised Jairus' daughter from the dead. She was about twelve years old at the time.
beginner.explanation: Jairus, a synagogue leader, begged Jesus to come heal his dying daughter. By the time Jesus arrived, she had died. Jesus took her hand and said, 'Little girl, get up!' and she immediately stood up.

beginner.question: When Jesus arrived at Jairus' house, He said, 'The child is not dead but asleep,' and the people laughed at Him.
beginner.explanation: Jesus told the mourners the girl was sleeping, not dead. They laughed at Him because they knew she was dead. But Jesus put them out of the room, took her hand, and raised her back to life.

beginner.question: Peter found a pearl inside a fish's mouth, just as Jesus had told him he would.
beginner.explanation: Peter found a coin — not a pearl — inside the fish's mouth. Jesus told Peter to cast a hook, and the first fish he caught had a four-drachma coin in its mouth, enough to pay the temple tax for both of them.

beginner.question: During the Transfiguration, Jesus' appearance changed — His face shone like the sun and His clothes became dazzling white.
beginner.explanation: On a high mountain, Jesus was transfigured before Peter, James, and John. His face shone like the sun, His clothes became as white as light, and Moses and Elijah appeared talking with Him.

beginner.question: During the Transfiguration, Abraham and David appeared and talked with Jesus.
beginner.explanation: It was Moses and Elijah — not Abraham and David — who appeared with Jesus during the Transfiguration. Moses represented the Law and Elijah represented the Prophets of the Old Testament.

beginner.question: Jesus rose from the dead on the third day after His crucifixion, just as He had predicted.
beginner.explanation: Jesus was crucified on Friday and rose from the dead on Sunday — the third day. He had told His disciples multiple times that He would be killed and raised on the third day.

beginner.question: The women who went to Jesus' tomb on Sunday morning found the stone still in place and Roman soldiers standing guard.
beginner.explanation: When the women arrived, they found the stone had already been rolled away and the tomb was empty. An angel told them that Jesus had risen from the dead, just as He had promised.

beginner.question: Mary Magdalene was the first person to see the risen Jesus after His resurrection, according to the Gospel of John.
beginner.explanation: Mary Magdalene was weeping at the empty tomb when Jesus appeared to her. She initially mistook Him for the gardener, but recognised Him when He said her name.

beginner.question: The disciple Thomas believed in the resurrection immediately when the other disciples told him about it.
beginner.explanation: Thomas refused to believe until he could see and touch Jesus' wounds himself. This earned him the nickname 'Doubting Thomas.' When Jesus appeared and invited him to touch His wounds, Thomas finally believed.

beginner.question: After His resurrection, Jesus appeared to His followers over a period of forty days before ascending to heaven.
beginner.explanation: Jesus showed Himself alive to the apostles by many convincing proofs during forty days, speaking to them about the kingdom of God before ascending to heaven.

beginner.question: At Pentecost, the Holy Spirit came upon the disciples and they began speaking in languages they had never learned.
beginner.explanation: On the day of Pentecost, the Holy Spirit filled the disciples. Tongues of fire appeared on each of them, and they miraculously spoke in foreign languages, amazing visitors from many nations.

beginner.question: At Pentecost, the Holy Spirit appeared as a pillar of cloud over the disciples.
beginner.explanation: The Holy Spirit at Pentecost appeared as tongues of fire on each disciple — not a pillar of cloud. A pillar of cloud was the sign God used to lead the Israelites in the Old Testament wilderness.

beginner.question: Saul (later called Paul) was travelling to Damascus to arrest Christians when Jesus appeared to him in a blinding light.
beginner.explanation: Saul was on his way to persecute Christians in Damascus when a bright light from heaven flashed around him. He fell to the ground and heard the voice of Jesus saying, 'Saul, Saul, why do you persecute me?'

beginner.question: After his encounter with Jesus on the road to Damascus, Saul (Paul) was blind for seven days.
beginner.explanation: Saul was blind for three days — not seven. During those three days in Damascus, he did not eat or drink. Then a disciple named Ananias came and prayed for him, and his sight was restored.

beginner.question: An angel of the Lord freed the apostle Peter from prison, causing his chains to fall off.
beginner.explanation: While Peter was sleeping in prison, chained between two soldiers, an angel appeared. The chains fell off Peter's wrists, and the angel led him past the guards and through an iron gate that opened by itself.

beginner.question: Noah sent out a dove from the ark, and it returned with an olive branch, showing that dry land had appeared after the flood.
beginner.explanation: After the floodwaters began receding, Noah sent out a dove. It came back with a freshly plucked olive leaf in its beak, telling Noah that trees were growing again and the waters were going down.

beginner.question: It rained for forty days and forty nights during the great flood in Noah's time.
beginner.explanation: The Bible says it rained for forty days and forty nights. The floodwaters rose and covered the entire earth, including the highest mountains, destroying all life except those aboard the ark.

beginner.question: In the desert, God only sent manna to feed the Israelites and did not provide any meat.
beginner.explanation: God sent both manna and quail. The manna appeared on the ground each morning, and God sent quail (birds) in the evening for meat. Together, they sustained the Israelites through the wilderness.

beginner.question: Moses threw his staff on the ground before Pharaoh and it turned into a snake.
beginner.explanation: This was one of the signs God gave Moses to perform before Pharaoh. When Moses threw his staff down, it became a snake. Pharaoh's magicians replicated the trick, but Moses' snake swallowed theirs.

beginner.question: God guided the Israelites through the desert with a pillar of cloud by day and a pillar of fire by night.
beginner.explanation: God provided constant guidance with a pillar of cloud during the day and a pillar of fire at night. These miraculous signs showed God's presence and allowed the Israelites to travel at any time.

beginner.question: The Israelites' clothing and sandals wore out quickly during their forty years in the desert.
beginner.explanation: Miraculously, the Israelites' clothes did not wear out and their sandals did not fall apart during the entire forty years in the wilderness. God preserved everything they needed throughout their journey.

beginner.question: Joshua prayed and God made the sun stand still in the sky so the Israelites could win a battle.
beginner.explanation: Joshua commanded the sun to stand still over the city of Gibeon, and God answered his prayer. The sun stopped in the middle of the sky for about a full day, giving the Israelites enough time to defeat their enemies.

beginner.question: Aaron's staff miraculously sprouted, budded, and produced grapes overnight.
beginner.explanation: Aaron's staff produced almonds — not grapes. When the staffs of all tribal leaders were placed in the Tabernacle overnight, only Aaron's sprouted buds, blossomed, and produced ripe almonds, confirming God's choice.

beginner.question: In the story of Balaam, God miraculously caused Balaam's horse to speak in a human voice.
beginner.explanation: It was Balaam's donkey — not a horse — that God caused to speak. The donkey saw an angel blocking the road and stopped, and when Balaam beat it, God opened the donkey's mouth to speak.

beginner.question: The prophet Elijah was fed by ravens that brought him bread and meat by the Kerith Ravine.
beginner.explanation: During a drought, God told Elijah to hide by the Kerith Ravine. Ravens miraculously brought him bread and meat every morning and evening, and he drank water from the brook.

beginner.question: The widow of Zarephath's flour and oil supply miraculously never ran out while Elijah stayed with her during a famine.
beginner.explanation: Elijah promised the widow that her jar of flour would not be used up and her jug of oil would not run dry until the drought ended. Just as the prophet said, the food lasted throughout the entire famine.

beginner.question: Elisha made a poisonous pot of stew safe to eat by adding salt to it.
beginner.explanation: Elisha added flour — not salt — to the poisoned stew. When the prophets cried out that there was 'death in the pot,' Elisha threw in flour and the stew became perfectly safe to eat.

beginner.question: Elisha made a heavy iron axe head float on the surface of the Jordan River.
beginner.explanation: When a man lost a borrowed iron axe head in the Jordan River, Elisha cut a stick and threw it into the water. The heavy iron axe head miraculously floated to the surface so the man could retrieve it.

beginner.question: Elisha raised the son of a Shunammite woman back to life after the boy died from a severe headache.
beginner.explanation: The boy had complained of a headache while in the fields and died on his mother's lap. His mother sought out Elisha, who prayed and lay on the child. The boy sneezed seven times and opened his eyes.

beginner.question: Jesus used ten stone water jars to turn water into wine at the wedding in Cana.
beginner.explanation: Jesus used six stone water jars — not ten. Each jar held twenty to thirty gallons. The servants filled them with water, and when they drew some out, it had become the finest wine at the feast.

beginner.question: The food that Jesus used to feed the 5,000 originally came from a young boy in the crowd.
beginner.explanation: A boy in the crowd had five small barley loaves and two small fish. The disciple Andrew brought the boy to Jesus, who blessed the food and miraculously multiplied it to feed the entire crowd.

beginner.question: Jesus also performed a separate miracle where He fed 4,000 people in addition to the feeding of the 5,000.
beginner.explanation: The feeding of the 4,000 was a separate miracle from the feeding of the 5,000. In this case, Jesus used seven loaves and a few small fish to feed the crowd, and seven baskets of leftovers were collected.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a man who had been born blind by putting mud on his eyes and telling him to wash in the Pool of Siloam.
beginner.explanation: Jesus made mud with His saliva and dirt, put it on the blind man's eyes, and told him to wash in the Pool of Siloam. The man obeyed, washed, and came back able to see for the first time in his life.

beginner.question: When Jesus healed the ten lepers, all ten came back to thank Him.
beginner.explanation: Only one of the ten healed lepers came back to thank Jesus — and he was a Samaritan. Jesus asked, 'Were not all ten cleansed? Where are the other nine?' showing His disappointment at their ingratitude.

beginner.question: Four friends lowered a paralysed man through a hole in the roof to get him to Jesus because the house was too crowded.
beginner.explanation: The crowd around Jesus was so thick that the four friends could not get through the door. So they climbed onto the roof, made an opening, and lowered the paralysed man on his mat right in front of Jesus.

beginner.question: A woman who had been bleeding for twelve years was healed when she touched Jesus' robe.
beginner.explanation: The woman had suffered for twelve years and spent all her money on doctors. She believed if she could just touch Jesus' clothes, she would be healed. When she touched His robe, her bleeding immediately stopped.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a man at the Pool of Bethesda who had been unable to walk for twelve years.
beginner.explanation: The man at the Pool of Bethesda had been an invalid for thirty-eight years — not twelve. Jesus simply told him, 'Get up! Pick up your mat and walk,' and the man was instantly healed.

beginner.question: Jesus cast out demons from a man and sent them into a herd of pigs, which then rushed into the sea and drowned.
beginner.explanation: The demons, called 'Legion,' begged Jesus to send them into a nearby herd of pigs rather than into the abyss. Jesus allowed it, and the entire herd rushed down the steep bank into the sea and drowned.

beginner.question: Jesus healed a deaf man by saying the word 'Ephphatha,' which means 'Be opened.'
beginner.explanation: Jesus put His fingers in the man's ears, touched his tongue, looked up to heaven, and said 'Ephphatha!' which is an Aramaic word meaning 'Be opened!' The man's ears were opened and he could speak clearly.

beginner.question: Jesus raised a widow's son from the dead in the town of Capernaum.
beginner.explanation: Jesus raised the widow's son from the dead in the town of Nain — not Capernaum. He stopped the funeral procession, touched the coffin, and said, 'Young man, I say to you, get up!' and the dead man sat up.

beginner.question: When Jesus was baptised in the Jordan River, the Holy Spirit descended on Him in the form of a dove.
beginner.explanation: After Jesus was baptised, heaven opened and the Holy Spirit descended on Him in bodily form like a dove. A voice from heaven said, 'This is my Son, whom I love, with whom I am well pleased.'

beginner.question: A star appeared in the sky when Jesus was born, and wise men from the East followed it to find Him.
beginner.explanation: When Jesus was born in Bethlehem, a special star appeared that guided the Magi (wise men) from the East. They followed the star to Bethlehem, where they found the child Jesus and presented Him with gifts.

beginner.question: The angel Michael appeared to Mary to tell her she would give birth to the Son of God.
beginner.explanation: It was the angel Gabriel — not Michael — who appeared to Mary in Nazareth. Gabriel told her she had found favour with God and would conceive a son by the Holy Spirit, to be named Jesus.

beginner.question: At the moment of Jesus' death on the cross, the curtain in the temple was torn in two from top to bottom.
beginner.explanation: When Jesus breathed His last, the thick temple curtain was miraculously torn in two from top to bottom. This curtain had separated the Holy of Holies, and its tearing symbolised open access to God for all people.

beginner.question: Jesus ascended to heaven and a cloud hid Him from the disciples' sight.
beginner.explanation: After forty days of post-resurrection appearances, Jesus was taken up before His disciples' eyes, and a cloud hid Him from their sight. Two angels told them He would return the same way He had gone.

beginner.question: Peter and John healed a lame man at the temple gate called Beautiful by giving him silver and gold.
beginner.explanation: Peter specifically said, 'Silver or gold I do not have, but what I do have I give you. In the name of Jesus Christ of Nazareth, walk.' The man was healed by the power of Jesus' name, not by money.

beginner.question: In the miraculous catch of fish after the resurrection, the disciples caught exactly 153 large fish.
beginner.explanation: When Jesus told the disciples to cast their net on the right side of the boat, they caught 153 large fish. Despite the enormous catch, the net was not torn. This miracle helped them recognise the risen Lord.

beginner.question: An earthquake happened at the moment of Jesus' death on the cross.
beginner.explanation: When Jesus died, the earth shook, rocks split, and the temple curtain was torn. The Roman centurion and soldiers guarding Jesus were terrified and said, 'Surely He was the Son of God!'

beginner.question: Paul and Silas were freed from prison by an earthquake while they were singing hymns at midnight.
beginner.explanation: While Paul and Silas were praying and singing hymns in prison at midnight, a violent earthquake shook the prison. All the doors flew open and every prisoner's chains came loose.

beginner.question: The apostle Paul was bitten by a venomous snake on the island of Malta but suffered no harm.
beginner.explanation: While Paul was gathering firewood, a viper fastened itself on his hand. He shook it off into the fire and suffered no ill effects. The islanders expected him to die but were amazed when nothing happened.

beginner.question: The apostle Peter raised a woman named Tabitha (Dorcas) from the dead in the city of Joppa.
beginner.explanation: Peter was called to Joppa when Tabitha died. He sent everyone out of the room, knelt and prayed, then said, 'Tabitha, get up.' She opened her eyes, and Peter presented her alive to the believers.

beginner.question: When the Israelites were bitten by poisonous snakes in the desert, Moses made a bronze snake on a pole. Anyone who looked at it was healed.
beginner.explanation: God told Moses to make a bronze snake and put it on a pole. Anyone who had been bitten by a snake could look at the bronze snake and live. This became a symbol of salvation and healing.

beginner.question: Elijah raised the son of the widow of Zarephath by stretching himself over the boy three times and praying.
beginner.explanation: When the widow's son died, Elijah carried him upstairs, stretched himself over the boy three times, and cried out to God. The Lord heard Elijah's prayer, and the boy's life returned to him.

beginner.question: Elisha crossed the Jordan River by striking the water with Elijah's cloak, and the water divided.
beginner.explanation: After Elijah was taken to heaven, Elisha picked up his fallen cloak. He struck the Jordan River with it, the water divided to the left and right, and Elisha walked across on dry ground.

beginner.question: When the Jordan River parted for the Israelites, the priests carrying the Ark crossed first and waited on the far bank.
beginner.explanation: The priests did not cross first — they stood in the middle of the dry riverbed while all the Israelites passed by. Only after everyone had crossed did the priests come up from the riverbed.

beginner.question: Naaman the army commander had to wash five times in the Jordan River to be healed of his leprosy.
beginner.explanation: Naaman had to wash seven times — not five — in the Jordan River, as Elisha instructed. After dipping himself seven times, Naaman's skin was completely restored and became like that of a young boy.

beginner.question: Light was the very first thing God created, according to the Book of Genesis.
beginner.explanation: God said, 'Let there be light,' and there was light. This was the first act of creation on the first day. God saw that the light was good and separated it from the darkness.

beginner.question: God created humans on the fifth day of creation.
beginner.explanation: God created humans on the sixth day — not the fifth. On the fifth day, God created sea creatures and birds. On the sixth day, He made land animals and then created man and woman in His own image.

beginner.question: God created the first woman, Eve, from one of Adam's ribs.
beginner.explanation: God caused Adam to fall into a deep sleep, took one of his ribs, and formed it into Eve. When Adam saw her, he said she was bone of his bones and flesh of his flesh.

beginner.question: The burning bush that Moses saw was located near Mount Sinai.
beginner.explanation: The burning bush was at Mount Horeb — not Mount Sinai, though some scholars identify them as the same location or nearby. The Bible specifically says Moses was at 'Horeb, the mountain of God.'

beginner.question: One of the ten plagues of Egypt was a plague of frogs that covered the entire land.
beginner.explanation: The second plague brought frogs everywhere in Egypt — in their houses, bedrooms, ovens, and kneading troughs. The frogs were so overwhelming that Pharaoh begged Moses to pray for their removal.

beginner.question: The ninth plague of Egypt brought total darkness over the land for seven days.
beginner.explanation: The darkness lasted three days — not seven. During those three days, no one in Egypt could see anyone else or leave their home, but the Israelites had light where they lived.

beginner.question: Locusts were one of the ten plagues that God sent upon Egypt.
beginner.explanation: The eighth plague was a massive swarm of locusts that devoured every plant and fruit in the land of Egypt. The locusts covered the ground so completely that it appeared black.

beginner.question: Samson defeated a thousand Philistine soldiers using the jawbone of a donkey.
beginner.explanation: The Spirit of the Lord came upon Samson powerfully. He picked up a fresh jawbone of a donkey and struck down a thousand Philistine men with it, demonstrating his God-given supernatural strength.

beginner.question: Samson's hair was cut while he was awake and fighting Delilah.
beginner.explanation: Samson's hair was cut while he was asleep. Delilah lulled Samson to sleep on her lap, and then she called a man to shave off the seven braids of his hair, and his strength left him.

beginner.question: When Elisha's servant was afraid of the enemy army surrounding them, God opened his eyes to see hills full of horses and chariots of fire.
beginner.explanation: Elisha told his frightened servant, 'Those who are with us are more than those who are with them.' When God opened the servant's eyes, he saw the hills filled with God's heavenly army of horses and chariots of fire.

beginner.question: Daniel survived the lions' den because he fought off the lions with a sword.
beginner.explanation: Daniel did not fight the lions — God sent an angel who shut the lions' mouths so they could not harm him. Daniel was found completely unharmed the next morning because he trusted in God.

beginner.question: Before Jonah was swallowed by the great fish, the sailors threw him overboard during a violent storm.
beginner.explanation: Jonah told the sailors to throw him into the sea because he knew the storm was his fault for running from God. When they threw him overboard, the sea immediately became calm.

beginner.question: After Jonah was spit out by the great fish, he still refused to go to Nineveh.
beginner.explanation: After his ordeal inside the fish, Jonah obeyed God and went to Nineveh to preach. The people of Nineveh heard his message, repented of their sins, and God spared the city from destruction.

beginner.question: Jesus' mother Mary was the one who told Him that the wine had run out at the wedding in Cana.
beginner.explanation: Mary noticed the wine had run out and told Jesus about the problem. Even though Jesus said His time had not yet come, Mary told the servants to do whatever He instructed, and He performed the miracle.

beginner.question: The master of the banquet at the wedding in Cana knew that Jesus had turned water into wine.
